title: Phase B: NUFFT-based dll_lindblad_op_time (circuit-faithful TimeDomain dissipator)
notes:
## Goal

Replace the explicit Nt × n² `cis()` triple loop in `dll_lindblad_op_time`
with a single FINUFFT type-3 evaluation at ω = 0. The Riemann sum being
computed is unchanged — only the algorithm for evaluating the DFT changes.
Quadrature error structure stays exactly Eq. 3.15 of the DLL paper.

## What's the same Riemann sum

DLL Eq. 3.4 (third form) on the simulator's truncated time grid:

    L_a[i,j] = A_eb[i,j] · τ · Σ_m f^a(t_m) · e^{i(λ_i − λ_j) · t_m}

This is `out[i,j] = Σ_m f(t_m) · e^{i(λ_i−λ_j) t_m}` evaluated at the
non-uniform Bohr-frequency targets {λ_i − λ_j}. FINUFFT type-3 computes
exactly this sum to ε=1e-12, in ~O(N_t log N_t + n² log(1/ε)) ops.

The existing `_prepare_oft_nufft_prefactors` (src/nufft.jl:19) already
accepts an `AbstractFilter` (filters.jl:64) — `time_kernel.(Ref(filter), …)`
on `DLLGaussianFilter` returns the correct complex-valued f(t_m).

## Two integration options

(a) **Direct reuse**: call `_prepare_oft_nufft_prefactors` with
    `energy_labels = [0.0]`. The existing routine then computes
    `out[u] = Σ_m f(t_m) · cis(0 · t_m) · cis(target_u · t_m) = Σ_m f(t_m) · cis(target_u t_m)`,
    which is exactly the DLL slice. One slice in the prefactor stack.

(b) **Dedicated routine**: add a `_prepare_dll_nufft_lindblad` that skips
    the (ω = 0) `cis(-ω · t_m)` modulation entirely. Saves the per-(omega,m)
    elementwise multiply of the existing routine. Marginal savings since
    the cost is dominated by the FINUFFT exec, but cleaner code.

Recommendation: start with (a) for minimal infrastructure churn; profile
afterwards to see if (b) is worth it.

## Tasks

1. Read DLL paper Eq. 3.4 (third form, Eq. 3.13 grid) + §3.2 + Prop. 16
   (Eq. 3.15 error bound). Confirm the time-grid Riemann sum and the τ
   weighting match the simulator's `t_m = -Mτ + mτ` convention.

2. Extend `_precompute_data` for `Config{Lindbladian, TimeDomain, DLL}` in
   `src/furnace_utensils.jl` to build a one-slice NUFFT prefactor at ω=0:
   - Add `oft_nufft_prefactor_omega_zero` (or similar) field to the
     returned NamedTuple.
   - Use `_prepare_oft_nufft_prefactors(ham.bohr_freqs, oft_time_labels,
     [0.0], filter; eps=1e-12, nthreads=1)` (option a) or write the
     dedicated routine (option b).

3. Update `_jump_contribution!` for `Config{Lindbladian, TimeDomain, DLL}`
   in `src/jump_workers.jl`:
   - Replace `dll_lindblad_op_time(jump, hamiltonian, time_labels, filter, t0)`
     with the elementwise multiply `L_a = (τ · prefactor_omega_zero) .* A_eb`,
     mirroring CKG's structure (`@. jump_oft = jump.in_eigenbasis * prefactor`).
   - Verify τ accounting: prefactor_omega_zero has unit `[time]^{-1}` after
     the FINUFFT scaling, then multiplied by τ here (or vice versa — check
     against current routine output).

4. Keep `dll_lindblad_op_time` in src/dll.jl as a reference / fallback for
   tests, but mark it as superseded by the NUFFT path. Don't delete — useful
   for cross-checking ε=1e-12.

5. Update `test/test_dll_dissipator.jl`:
   - Cross-check NUFFT path vs explicit Riemann sum at ε=1e-12.
   - Bohr↔Time agreement at high N_t (within Eq. 3.15 bound).
   - Gibbs FP check at β ∈ {1, 5, 10}.
   - KMS-DB via `verify_detailed_balance`.

6. Run targeted tests: `test/test_dll_dissipator.jl`,
   `test/test_dll_coherent.jl`, `test/test_dll_kms_db.jl`.

7. Atomic commits:
   - `feat(dll): TimeDomain dissipator via FINUFFT slice at ω=0 (reuse CKG NUFFT)`
   - `test(dll-dissipator): cross-check NUFFT vs explicit Riemann sum`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] DLL TimeDomain dissipator uses FINUFFT (not explicit triple loop).
- [ ] Cross-check NUFFT vs explicit `dll_lindblad_op_time` agrees to 1e-12.
- [ ] Bohr↔Time consistency within Eq. 3.15 bound.
- [ ] Gibbs FP `‖L[σ_β]‖_F < 1e-10` at β ∈ {1, 5, 10}.
- [ ] KMS-DB passes at β ∈ {1, 5, 10}.
- [ ] DLL TimeDomain dissipator-only build < 0.05 s at n=5, β=5 (was 0.4 s).
- [ ] Atomic commits per julia-code.md.

## Files

- `src/furnace_utensils.jl` — extend DLL TimeDomain `_precompute_data`
- `src/jump_workers.jl` — `_jump_contribution!` for `Config{Lindbladian, TimeDomain, DLL}`
- `src/nufft.jl` — only if option (b) chosen
- `test/test_dll_dissipator.jl` — extend cross-checks
- `src/dll.jl` — keep `dll_lindblad_op_time` as reference

## References

- Existing NUFFT: `src/nufft.jl::_prepare_oft_nufft_prefactors`
- Filter abstraction supports DLL: `src/filters.jl:64`
- DLL paper Eq. 3.4, 3.13, 3.15, Prop. 16
Phase B complete (commits 802a08b, d29c1cc). NUFFT slice at ω=0 replaces explicit Nt × n² cis() triple loop in dll_lindblad_op_time. Setup amortised in _precompute_data via _prepare_oft_nufft_prefactors with [0.0]. _jump_contribution! uses single elementwise multiply per jump. Cross-check at ε=1e-12 (3 betas × 9 jumps on n=3 fixture). DLL TimeDomain dissipator-only: 0.456s → 0.046s (10× faster, target <0.05s met). End-to-end DLL TimeDomain still 167s — bottleneck is dll_coherent_op_time, addressed by Phase C.
close_reason: NUFFT-based dissipator integrated; dissipator-only build now 0.046s (target <0.05s met).

title: Phase A: Closed-form dll_coherent_op_bohr (idealized BohrDomain)
notes:
## Goal

Replace the O(|B_H|² · n³) brute-force loop in `dll_coherent_op_bohr` with the
closed-form O(n³) expression. BohrDomain is idealized — no quadrature errors —
so this is a pure algebraic identity, no physics tradeoff.

## Closed form (verified at machine precision)

In the eigenbasis of H, with `f̂(ν) := freq_kernel(filter, ν)`:

    G = (1/2i) · T .* Σ_a (M^a† M^a)
        T[i,j]   = tanh(β(λ_j − λ_i)/4)
        M^a[k,j] = f̂(λ_k − λ_j) · A^a[k,j]

Derivation (DLL Eq. 3.5 + Eq. 3.7 first form): in the eigenbasis,
A^a_ν[k,j] is supported on (k,j) with ν = λ_k − λ_j, so summing (ν,ν')
collapses each pair into a single (k,i,j) term, and the tanh factor
evaluates to tanh(β(λ_j − λ_i)/4) — independent of k, hence the Hadamard.

Self-adjointness: T anti-Hermitian (T[j,i] = −T[i,j]), Σ M^a† M^a Hermitian,
so (1/2i) · T .* Hermitian is Hermitian. Matches Theorem 10.

Verified by `scripts/scratch_dll_coherent_closedform.jl` at n=5, β=5:
  - residual ‖G_brute − G_closedform‖_op = 4.5e-17
  - relative error = 8.2e-16
  - Hermiticity ‖G − G†‖_op = 0
  - 339,000× speedup

## Tasks

1. Read DLL paper Eq. 3.4 + Eq. 3.5 + Eq. 3.7 + Theorem 10 (Sec. 2.5).
   Confirm the derivation in this beads note matches the paper exactly,
   especially the operator ordering in Eq. 3.7.

2. Replace the body of `dll_coherent_op_bohr` in `src/dll.jl` with the
   closed-form expression. Keep the same signature.

3. Update the docstring to reflect the closed-form, citing Theorem 10
   for self-adjointness and noting the algebraic equivalence.

4. Update `test/test_dll_coherent.jl`:
   - Existing tests should still pass at machine precision (the function
     is bit-equivalent up to floating-point reordering).
   - Add explicit Hermiticity test ‖G − G†‖_op == 0 (the closed form
     gives EXACTLY zero, unlike the brute-force which is ~1e-17).
   - Add KMS-DB check at β ∈ {1, 5, 10} via `verify_detailed_balance`.
   - Add Gibbs FP check `‖L[σ_β]‖_F < 1e-12` at β ∈ {1, 5, 10}.

5. Run `julia --project test/test_dll_coherent.jl` and the targeted DLL
   test files (per `feedback_targeted_tests_during_integration.md`).

6. Atomic commits:
   - `feat(dll): closed-form dll_coherent_op_bohr (Eq. 3.7) — O(n³) instead of O(|B_H|² n³)`
   - Optional: `test(dll-coherent): add Hermiticity, KMS-DB, Gibbs FP at β∈{1,5,10}`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Closed-form replaces brute-force loop in `dll_coherent_op_bohr`.
- [ ] All existing `test/test_dll_*.jl` tests pass.
- [ ] New tests added: exact Hermiticity, KMS-DB at β ∈ {1, 5, 10}, Gibbs FP.
- [ ] DLL BohrDomain end-to-end build < 0.5 s at n=5, β=5 (was 18 s).
- [ ] Atomic commits per julia-code.md.

## Files

- `src/dll.jl` (rewrite `dll_coherent_op_bohr` body)
- `test/test_dll_coherent.jl` (extend cross-checks)
- `scripts/scratch_dll_coherent_closedform.jl` (already exists as proof)

## References

- Closed-form proof: `scripts/scratch_dll_coherent_closedform.jl`
- Benchmark before: `scripts/scratch_benchmark_dll_vs_ckg.jl`
- DLL paper Sec. 2.5 (Theorem 10) + Sec. 3.1 (Eq. 3.4–3.7)

All 1169 existing DLL tests pass. n=5,β=5: dll_coherent_op_bohr 17.9s → 0.0001s (~180,000×); DLL BohrDomain end-to-end 18.0s → 0.025s (720× speedup, well below 0.5s target). Hermiticity ‖G−G†‖_op == 0 exactly for closed form. Tests refactored from n=2 toy → n=3 disordered Heisenberg fixture per user request.

title: DLL performance refactor: fast Bohr (closed-form G), fast Time (NUFFT + faithful Riemann sum)
notes:
## Goal

Make DLL Lindbladian construction fast in BohrDomain (idealized) AND TimeDomain (circuit-faithful).
Target: **DLL TimeDomain should be faster than CKG TimeDomain** (no outer ω-loop in DLL).

## Current state (n=5, β=5, 2 jumps; src/dll.jl + src/jump_workers.jl)

| construction | wall-time | bottleneck |
|---|---|---|
| CKG KMS TimeDomain | 1.1 s | NUFFT-prefactor + 67-ω inner loop |
| DLL BohrDomain | 18.0 s (16× slower!) | `dll_coherent_op_bohr` 17.9 s |
| DLL TimeDomain | 167 s (149× slower!) | `dll_coherent_op_time` 166 s |

Verified by `scripts/scratch_benchmark_dll_vs_ckg.jl`.

## Diagnosis

1. `dll_coherent_op_bohr` does an O(|B_H|² · n³) double loop where an O(n³)
   closed form exists: `G = (1/2i) · T .* (M† M)` with `M[k,j]=f̂(λ_k-λ_j)·A[k,j]`,
   `T[i,j]=tanh(β(λ_j-λ_i)/4)`. Verified at machine precision (rel err 8e-16,
   339,000× speedup at n=5) in `scripts/scratch_dll_coherent_closedform.jl`.

2. `dll_lindblad_op_time` does Nt × n² explicit `cis` calls per jump instead
   of using the existing FINUFFT infrastructure. The DLL Lindblad operator
   at ω=0 IS structurally a single energy slice of CKG's NUFFT prefactor
   stack — `_prepare_oft_nufft_prefactors` already accepts `DLLGaussianFilter`
   (filters.jl:64), it's just not called.

3. `dll_coherent_op_time` tabulates ĝ(ν,ν') on an internal arbitrary
   (ν,ν') grid centered at -1/β with halfwidth 12/β, then 2D-FFTs to time
   grid, then does Nt² × n³ matmuls. The internal grid is a Julia-numerics
   artifact NOT in the DLL paper — Ding–Li–Lin 2024 §3.2 + Prop. 16 + Remark 17
   compute g(t,t') analytically from the closed-form Gevrey filter and bound
   the quadrature error purely in time-domain (Eq. 3.13/3.17). The "rounding
   Hamiltonian" used by CKG to handle frequency-grid quadrature is explicitly
   NOT needed for DLL.

## Plan (3 phases, atomic commits per phase)

### Phase A: Closed-form `dll_coherent_op_bohr` (idealized BohrDomain)
- Read DLL paper Eq. 3.4–3.7 + Theorem 10 (Sec. 2.5 self-adjointness of G).
- Replace brute-force loop with closed form `G = (1/2i) T .* Σ_a M^a† M^a`.
- Tests: agreement with brute force at machine precision; Hermiticity;
  Gibbs FP `‖L[σ_β]‖_F < 1e-12`; KMS-DB via existing
  `verify_detailed_balance` at β ∈ {1, 5, 10}.
- No CKG integration — Bohr is DLL-only.
- Expected speedup: ~10⁵× at n=5; ~10⁴× at n=4.

### Phase B: NUFFT-based `dll_lindblad_op_time` (circuit-faithful TimeDomain dissipator)
- Read DLL paper §3.2 + Prop. 16 (time-grid Riemann-sum error bound Eq. 3.15).
- Reuse `_prepare_oft_nufft_prefactors` from src/nufft.jl — already supports
  `DLLGaussianFilter` via `time_kernel.(Ref(filter), …)` on filters.jl:64.
- Two integration options to evaluate:
  - (a) Build NUFFT prefactor stack with `energy_labels = [0.0]` (one slice).
  - (b) Add a dedicated `_prepare_dll_nufft_prefactors_omega_zero` that skips
    the inner `cis(-ω·t_m)` modulation since ω=0 (saves N ops per setup).
- After NUFFT slice is in `precomputed_data`, `_jump_contribution!` for
  `Config{Lindbladian, TimeDomain, DLL}` becomes a single elementwise multiply
  (mirroring CKG TimeDomain's structure).
- Tests: agreement with current `dll_lindblad_op_time` at ε=1e-12; Bohr↔Time
  at high N_t; Gibbs FP; KMS-DB at β ∈ {1, 5, 10}.
- Same algorithm — only the DFT is computed via FFT-NUFFT instead of explicit
  triple loop. Quadrature error structure unchanged (Eq. 3.15).
- Expected speedup: ~30× per jump; eliminates the 0.4 s/jump overhead.

### Phase C: Faithful + fast `dll_coherent_op_time` (preserves Eq. 3.17 error)
- Read DLL paper §3.2 + §3.5 + Prop. 16 (Eq. 3.17 error bound).
- Eliminate the internal (ν,ν') tabulation grid — it has no analog in the
  paper. Compute g^a(t_m, t_n) directly from a closed-form / 1D high-precision
  quadrature using the DLL filter formulas (the Gevrey-tail decay makes this
  a small fixed-cost per (m,n) pair).
- Refactor the time-grid Riemann sum:
  `G[i,j] = τ² Σ_k A[i,k] A[k,j] · Q(λ_i-λ_k, λ_k-λ_j)` where
  `Q(α,γ) = Σ_{m,n} g(t_m,t_n) e^{iα t_n + iγ t_m}` is a 2D type-3 NUFFT of
  the same `g_tt` table — same Riemann sum, only the DFT is fast.
- The error structure becomes EXACTLY Eq. 3.17 of the paper, controlled by
  (M, τ) = (num_energy_bits, t0). Internal grid drops out → more faithful
  to the paper, not less.
- Tests: agreement with closed-form Bohr `dll_coherent_op_bohr` at high N_t
  (the time-grid Riemann sum should converge to the Bohr limit);
  Gibbs FP; KMS-DB at β ∈ {1, 5, 10}; Bohr↔Time at high N_t to within the
  Eq. 3.17 bound.
- Expected speedup: ~10³× per jump while preserving the physical error.
- TARGET: total DLL TimeDomain build < CKG TimeDomain build.

## Per-phase workflow

For each phase:
1. Read the relevant DLL paper sections (don't infer from CKG analogues — see
   `feedback_dll_paper_first.md`).
2. Write/update prototype script in `scripts/scratch_dll_*.jl` with verifications.
3. Spawn `physics-checker` on prototype if needed.
4. Integrate into `src/dll.jl` (and `src/jump_workers.jl` / `src/furnace_utensils.jl`
   for Phase B).
5. Run targeted tests (`test/test_dll_*.jl`) per
   `feedback_targeted_tests_during_integration.md`.
6. Run full test suite at end of phase.
7. Atomic commits per `julia-code.md`.

## Cross-checks every phase must pass

- DLL Bohr vs DLL Time: ‖L_Bohr − L_Time‖_op below the Eq. 3.15/3.17 bound at
  the chosen (M, τ).
- KMS-DB: `verify_detailed_balance(L, σ_β; atol=1e-10)` returns true at
  β ∈ {1, 5, 10} (see `feedback_beta_test_values.md`).
- Gibbs FP: `‖L[σ_β]‖_F < 1e-10` for Lindbladian-domain checks.
- Kossakowski skew-symmetry α(ν,ν') = α(-ν',-ν)·e^{-β(ν+ν')/2} preserved
  (see `feedback_kossakowski_skew_symmetry_check.md`).
- Targeted regressions in `test/test_dll_coherent.jl`,
  `test/test_dll_dissipator.jl`, `test/test_dll_kms_db.jl`,
  `test/test_dll_kossakowski.jl`.

## Files to touch

- `src/dll.jl` (Phases A, C)
- `src/jump_workers.jl` (Phase B integration)
- `src/furnace_utensils.jl` (Phase B precompute)
- `src/nufft.jl` (Phase B if (b) option chosen)
- `test/test_dll_coherent.jl`, `test/test_dll_dissipator.jl` (all phases)
- `scripts/scratch_dll_*` for prototypes

## Reference materials

- DLL paper: `supplementary-informations/Ding et al. - 2024 - Efficient quantum Gibbs samplers with Kubo--Martin.pdf`
- Existing benchmark: `scripts/scratch_benchmark_dll_vs_ckg.jl`
- Closed-form prototype: `scripts/scratch_dll_coherent_closedform.jl`
- DLL phase 51 notes: `.claude-memory/dll_implementation_phase51.md`
Epic complete. 3 phases, 5 commits (9259ec7, 9ea9bff, 802a08b, d29c1cc, 06c64d7). DLL BohrDomain build 18s → 0.013s (1380×); DLL TimeDomain build 167s → 0.26s (640×). DLL TimeDomain is now 3.5× faster than CKG TimeDomain at n=5,β=5 (target was tied/faster). Test count 3142 → 3171 (+29 new cross-check assertions). All cross-checks at FINUFFT precision (1e-12), Eq. 3.15 / 3.17 error structure verified.

title: DLL-5: KMS DB verification — Gibbs fixed-point + discriminant antihermitian part (Bohr exact, Time/Trotter quadrature/Trotter-bounded)
description:
Verify that the assembled DLL Lindbladian (dissipator + coherent term, from DLL-2 and DLL-3) is KMS-DB. Two checks:

**(a) Gibbs state is the unique fixed point** — necessary, not sufficient. Sanity check, fast to run.

**(b) Antihermitian part of the quantum discriminant is zero** — this is the real KMS-DB witness.
- BohrDomain: must be **exactly zero** (machine epsilon) — analytic.
- TimeDomain: should be controllably small, of the same order as CKG's quadrature error at matched (σ, β, OFT grid).
- TrotterDomain: should be controllably small, dominated by Trotter error at matched parameters.

**Touch points (CKG analogue)**
- `src/discriminant.jl` — already has `apply_discriminant!`, `materialize_discriminant`, `hermitian_antihermitian_split`, `discriminant_spectrum`, `verify_detailed_balance`. These should mostly work as-is on the DLL Lindbladian; this issue's job is to drive them with DLL inputs and produce a comparison report.
- `src/convergence.jl` — existing fixed-point / mixing convergence checks.

**Acceptance**
- Test in BohrDomain: ‖antiherm part of D_DLL‖ < 1e-12.
- Test in TimeDomain: at matched CKG/DLL parameters, the antiherm-part norm is within ~the same order as CKG's.
- Test in TrotterDomain: at matched parameters, antiherm-part norm scales like the Trotter step δ in the same way CKG's does.
- Gibbs state fixed-point check passes for all three domains.
- A diagnostic script under `scripts/` that produces the comparison table CKG vs DLL.

**Depends on** DLL-2 and DLL-3 being in `src/`.
close_reason: DLL-5 KMS-DBC verified at beta in {1,5,10} (3142 tests pass)

title: DLL-4: Extract DLL Kossakowski α^{DLL}_{ν1,ν2}; comparison API with CKG
description:
Compute and expose the DLL Kossakowski matrix α^{DLL}_{ν₁,ν₂} for the same Bohr-frequency grids the existing CKG code already walks. Goal: enable side-by-side comparison with CKG (DLL paper Fig. ~3 is the visual target).

**Touch points (CKG analogue)**
- `src/bohr_domain.jl` — CKG already has `create_alpha`, `create_alpha_gns`, `create_alpha_gauss`, `_pick_alpha_kms`, `_pick_alpha_gns`, `check_alpha_skew_symmetry`. Add a DLL counterpart (`create_alpha_dll` or analogous), wired into the same dispatch tree introduced in DLL-2.
- `scripts/plot_kossakowski_heatmaps.jl` — existing CKG-only plotter for thesis Fig. (epic `qf-fx2.3`). Extend to overlay / juxtapose CKG and DLL Kossakowskis on matched (n, β, σ, family) parameters.

**Acceptance**
- A single API call returns the DLL Kossakowski as a `(num_ν × num_ν)` matrix on the Bohr grid.
- Skew-symmetry / Hermiticity properties of α^{DLL} match what the DLL paper proves.
- Sanity tests on n=2,3 systems.
- A CKG-vs-DLL comparison script exists (under `scripts/`) producing a side-by-side plot at matched parameters.
- `physics-checker`: verify α^{DLL} scales correctly with β, σ.

**Note** This issue depends on DLL-1 (filter abstraction) and benefits from DLL-2's type/dispatch decisions but does not strictly require DLL-2 to be merged.
close_reason: DLL-4 Kossakowski + KMS skew-sym verified (3116 tests pass)

title: DLL-3: DLL coherent (Hamiltonian-correction) term — Bohr / Time / Trotter
description:
Implement the DLL coherent term in all three domains. This is the structurally trickiest piece because **TimeDomain does not decouple into two independent Fourier transforms** the way CKG's coherent term does (CKG separates b₊ / b₋ via two Fouriers; DLL does not).

**Domain-by-domain**
- **BohrDomain**: form is only mildly different from CKG. Cross-check against the DLL paper expression and against `src/bohr_domain.jl::create_f`, `create_f_gauss`, `_pick_alpha_*`.
- **TimeDomain**: rederive carefully — no `b_+ ⋆ b_-` factorisation. Likely a single time-convolution with a DLL-specific kernel.
- **TrotterDomain**: TimeDomain expression discretised onto the Trotter grid.

**Touch points (CKG analogue)**
- `src/coherent.jl` — CKG builds `B_a` via `b_+`, `b_-`, `t0`, with optional GQSP polynomial path (`_gqsp_block_encoding_alpha`, `_gqsp_apply_polynomial`). Reuse the GQSP machinery if the DLL coherent term admits a similar block-encoding wrap; otherwise build a parallel path.
- `src/bohr_domain.jl::create_f`, `create_f_gauss` — CKG's Bohr-domain `f(ν₁,ν₂) = tanh(-β(ν₁-ν₂)/4) α(ν₁,ν₂)/(2i)` form.
- `src/structs.jl::Config` — add any DLL coherent-term-specific knobs (parallel to `with_gqsp`, `gqsp_degree`).

**Acceptance**
- BohrDomain: closed-form B_DLL agrees with the DLL paper and is Hermitian.
- TimeDomain: numerical B_DLL agrees with the BohrDomain form within OFT/quadrature error at matched parameters.
- TrotterDomain: agrees with TimeDomain within Trotter error.
- Tests cover all three domains; Hermiticity check passes.
- `physics-checker`: confirm the operator scales correctly with β and σ.
notes:
DLL-3 standalone prototype complete: scripts/scratch_dll_coherent.jl. Tests pass for β ∈ {1, 5, 10}. Bohr↔Time op-norm consistency ~1e-10 (well below 1e-4 tol), Gibbs FP residuals ~1e-11 (Time) and ~1e-16 (Bohr). Algebraic surprise: paper Eq. 3.7 third equality has typo 'A^a(t) A^a(t')' — re-derivation from Eq. 3.6 + Bohr decomp gives 'A^a(t') A^a(t)'. Confirmed numerically (only the swapped order matches Bohr form to machine precision). g^a(t,t') confirmed full-rank (rank = 18 of 84×84) — DLL coherent kernel does NOT factorise (in contrast to CKG b_-(t)·b_+(t')).
Final output file: /Users/bence/code/QuantumFurnace.jl/scripts/scratch_dll_coherent.jl. Standalone (no src/ or test/ touched). Run: julia --project scripts/scratch_dll_coherent.jl. Exit 0.
Starting integration of DLL coherent G into src/dll.jl, src/coherent.jl, and tests
close_reason: DLL-3 coherent term G integrated and tested (2074 tests pass, 16 new)

title: DLL-2: DLL dissipator — Lindbladian where OFTs are directly the Lindblad operators (no ω integral/sum)
description:
Build the DLL dissipator superoperator. The structural simplification vs CKG is **no energy integral / sum**: the OFT outputs (in the chosen domain) ARE the Lindblad operators. This avoids the outer ω-grid that CKG carries through `EnergyDomain` and the equivalent integration step in Time/Trotter.

**Where it lives**
- BohrDomain, TimeDomain, TrotterDomain — three implementations.
- Likely a new mode/dispatch alongside the current `KMS` / `GNS` types in `src/structs.jl`, or a sibling sampler family. Choose whichever keeps the type lattice sane and avoids a combinatorial blowup (`{KMS, GNS} × {CKG, DLL}`). Pose the type design as the first sub-step of this issue.

**Touch points (CKG analogue)**
- `src/bohr_domain.jl` — CKG builds `α(ν1,ν2)` and convolves into the Lindbladian; for DLL the operators are simpler post-OFT.
- `src/time_domain.jl`, `src/trotter_domain.jl` — CKG has the time/Trotter dissipator that will share scaffolding with DLL minus the ω-loop.
- `src/furnace.jl`, `src/jump_workers.jl` — Lindbladian assembly. Identify the ω/quadrature loop that DLL skips and route around it cleanly.

**Acceptance**
- DLL dissipator constructible in BohrDomain, TimeDomain, TrotterDomain via the same `Config`-style entry point as CKG.
- A simple sanity check: trace-preservation (Φ†(I) = 0 in dual / Φ is CPTP) on small n=2 systems.
- Tests scale: ≥ a handful covering each domain.
- `code-verifier` runs full suite — no regression on CKG paths.
notes:
DLL-2 prototype completed: scripts/scratch_dll_dissipator.jl. All checks PASS at β=1.0 and β=5.0. Bohr↔Time op-norm residual ≤ 4.4e-16 (well below the 1e-4 plan target — quadrature is essentially exact). Bohr Gibbs fixed-point and trace-preservation at machine precision. DLL Kossakowski rank=1 confirmed (single non-zero SV ≈ ‖f̂(B_H)‖²); CKG Kossakowski rank=9 for the dim-4 system, sv decay 4.8 → 8.85e-4. SymmetricToyFilter (real, even) gives exact L_a = L_a† to ulp. No src/ or test/ changes.
starting integration of DLL dissipator into src/ (BohrDomain + TimeDomain only; TrotterDomain deferred)
DLL-2 integrated and tested. Two new commits on top of the 4 prior src/ commits: chore(scripts) for the prototype, test(dll) for the BohrDomain/TimeDomain test file with beta sweep over {1, 5, 10}. All 25 DLL dissipator tests pass; full suite 2058 tests in 2m44s, no CKG regressions. beta=10 stress level: existing test grid (n_eb=12, w0=0.05, half-grid t_max~62.8) is wide enough vs the DLL filter cutoff at beta=10 (~35.5).

title: DLL-1: Filter abstraction in OFTs — add Gevrey filter alongside Gaussian (Time/Trotter)
description:
Generalise the OFT machinery so the time-window filter is an input. Add the DLL Gevrey filter; keep the existing Gaussian as the CKG default. CKG behaviour must be byte-identical when the Gaussian filter is selected.

**Touch points (CKG today)**
- `src/ofts.jl` — `oft!` (Bohr/energy form), `time_oft!`, `trotter_oft!`. Today the Gaussian appears explicitly: `inv_4sigma2`, `exp(-time_labels^2 * sigma^2 - 1im * energy * time_labels)`. Lift the filter into a callable / strategy.
- `src/time_domain.jl` — `_truncate_time_labels_for_oft` uses Gaussian-specific truncation `cutoff = sqrt(log(prefactor / tolerance)) / sigma`. Generalise to per-filter truncation (Gevrey decays slower than Gaussian — the cutoff formula will differ).
- `src/trotter_domain.jl` — same pattern.
- `src/structs.jl::Config` — currently has `sigma`, `gaussian_parameters`, `with_gqsp`, `gqsp_degree`. Add a filter selector (e.g. `filter_kind::Symbol = :gaussian`, with `:gevrey` as the new option, plus any Gevrey-specific shape parameters).

**DLL Gevrey filter** — see Ding et al. 2024 paper for the explicit form (Section on efficient OFTs). Include its time-domain envelope, normalisation, and truncation rule (numerical tail bound analogous to the Gaussian's `exp(-t^2 σ^2)` tail).

**Acceptance**
- New `filter_kind=:gevrey` Config option works through `time_oft!` and `trotter_oft!`.
- CKG Gaussian path is unchanged at the bit level (compare against frozen reference outputs).
- Truncation respects a user-set tolerance for both filters.
- New tests: filter normalisation, sigma scaling, parity vs CKG when Gaussian is selected.
- Physics-checker pass: Gevrey filter is real, peaked at t=0, integrates to 1 (or matches DLL's stated normalisation).

**Reuse**
Existing OFT caches (`prefactors`, eigenbasis pre-multiplications) and the `_truncate_time_labels_for_oft` skeleton.

title: DLL Lindbladian: implement Ding et al. KMS-DB Gibbs sampler (Bohr / Time / Trotter)
description:
Implement the Ding et al. (2024) "DLL" Gibbs sampler — a KMS-DB Lindbladian whose efficient OFTs use a Gevrey filter rather than a Gaussian, and whose Lindblad operators are obtained directly from the OFTs without an energy integral / sum.

**Primary reference**: `supplementary-informations/Ding et al. - 2024 - Efficient quantum Gibbs samplers with Kubo--Martin.pdf` ("EFFICIENT QUANTUM GIBBS SAMPLERS WITH KUBO–MARTIN–SCHWINGER DETAILED BALANCE CONDITION").

**Goals (from user spec)**
1. Build TimeDomain and TrotterDomain OFTs with the Gevrey filter (DLL) alongside the existing Gaussian filter (CKG). Filter must be a generic input to the OFT routines; CKG and DLL coexist.
2. Construct the DLL Lindbladian superoperator. Key structural simplification vs CKG: **no energy integral / sum**. The post-OFT operators ARE the Lindblad operators.
3. Extract the DLL Kossakowski matrix α^{DLL}_{ν1,ν2} for direct comparison with the CKG Kossakowski (DLL paper Fig. ~3).
4. Implement DLL's coherent (Hamiltonian-correction) term in BohrDomain, TimeDomain, and TrotterDomain. Note the form is only mildly different in BohrDomain but **structurally different in TimeDomain**: DLL does not decouple into two independent Fourier transforms the way CKG does.
5. Verify KMS DB: (a) Gibbs state is the unique fixed point (necessary, not sufficient); (b) the antihermitian part of the quantum discriminant is exactly zero in BohrDomain and controllably small in Time/TrotterDomain, with errors of the same order as CKG at matched parameters (quadrature + Trotter).

**Engineering principles**
- Maximise reuse of existing CKG infrastructure: `src/ofts.jl`, `src/bohr_domain.jl`, `src/time_domain.jl`, `src/trotter_domain.jl`, `src/coherent.jl`, `src/discriminant.jl`, `src/structs.jl::Config`, `src/furnace.jl`. Generalise rather than duplicate where possible.
- Whenever a CKG helper takes a Gaussian implicitly, lift the filter to an argument so the same code path serves both samplers.
- Match CKG parameter conventions (`sigma`, `beta`, mode dispatch like `KMS`/`GNS`) so cross-sampler comparisons are apples-to-apples.

**Workflow**
- Implementation: `sci-coder`, then `code-integrator` to merge into `src/`.
- Verification after each phase: `code-verifier` (tests) and `physics-checker` (physical sanity).
- Work closely from the DLL paper; consult Chen et al. and the existing CKG code as cross-checks.

**Outlook (NOT part of this epic)**
After the Lindbladian construction and KMS-DB verification land and look healthy, a follow-up epic will add a DLL thermalizing simulation that evolves the density matrix. DLL does not use weak measurement, so the simulation strategy needs separate research.
notes:
**Paper-first rule (user feedback, 2026-04-30):** Before writing any function in this epic — filter, dissipator, coherent term, Kossakowski, KMS-DB check — open `supplementary-informations/Ding et al. - 2024 - Efficient quantum Gibbs samplers with Kubo--Martin.pdf` and follow the formula it gives. Do NOT infer from the CKG analogue and assume DLL is the same shape with a different filter. When delegating to sci-coder, repeat this instruction in the prompt. Cite equation/section numbers in code comments and draft writeups so the user can verify alignment. If the paper is ambiguous on a detail, surface the ambiguity rather than guessing.
User decision (2026-04-30): defer TrotterDomain for DLL-2..5; implement BohrDomain + TimeDomain only for now.
close_reason: All 5 DLL phases complete (BohrDomain + TimeDomain). TrotterDomain deferred per user scope.

title: Plot M3: Empirical mixing time vs σ smearing (KMS smooth Metropolis, fixed n,β,family; DLL placeholder)
notes:
Files: scripts/numerics_m3_sigma_sweep.jl, scripts/numerics_m3_plot.jl, drafts/figures/numerics/m3_*. Reuses M1's TrotterDomain Thermalize + estimate_mixing_time(model=:biexp, target_epsilon=1e-3, extrapolate=true) pipeline, sweeping σ at fixed (family, n, β). Family = XXX_zz (1D disordered, most numerically tame), n ∈ {4, 5}, β ∈ {2, 4, 8}, σ ∈ {0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.8, 1.5, 2.5, 5} / β (7 points). KMS smooth Metropolis (a=β/30, s=0.4). 2 × 3 × 7 = 42 sweep points. Bump num_energy_bits 12 → 13 for σ < 0.5/β.

Plot: log-log t_mix (y) vs σ (x), one curve per (n, β); vertical dashed reference line at σ = 0.8/β. Below main panel: secondary subpanel of r_offdiag(σ) = sum(|α[i,j]|², i≠j) / sum(|α[i,j]|²) computed via _pick_alpha (src/bohr_domain.jl) — derived axis showing how σ controls Kossakowski off-diagonal weight.

DLL placeholder: plot script loads m3_data_dll.json if exists, else skips (DLL implementation is future work per CLAUDE.md remaining-goals item 2).

Caveats (in caption): σ has multiple effects (OFT envelope, transition rate envelope, AND α off-diagonal weight). r_offdiag is a summary statistic, not an isolated control. Cross-construction (KMS vs DLL) is the cleaner test of 'do off-diagonal terms help mixing'.

Acceptance: r² ≥ 0.95 for ≥ 90% of points; m3_notes.md reports σ_min per (n, β) and r_offdiag at σ_min; total walltime ≤ 2 hours on 16 cores.

Plan: .planning/phases/48-thesis-numerics-plots/PLAN.md (Task 5).
DLL placeholder dropped (2026-05-02): TrotterDomain Thermalize for DLL is no longer planned. DLL mixing-time analysis now handled by separate epic qf-lkb (Lindbladian-action ODE integrator). title: Hamiltonian generation: 3 families (1D XXX+ZZ disordered, 1D clean+ε, 2D clean+ε) up to n=10 on laptop, n≥11 on cluster
notes:
Three families:
(1) 1D XXX + [[Z],[Z,Z]] disorder — find_ideal_heisenberg with batch_size=200, replaces legacy hamiltonians/heis_disordered_periodic_n*.bson with hamiltonians/heis_xxx_zzdisordered_periodic_n{3..10}.bson.
(2) 1D XXX clean + ε-disorder (Z, strength 1e-3) — lifts translation×Sz×S² degeneracies so nu_min ≳ 1e-4. Files: hamiltonians/heis_xxx_clean_periodic_n{3..10}.bson. Uses find_ideal_heisenberg with batch_size=500.
(3) 2D XXX square lattice, periodic both directions, clean + ε-disorder — REQUIRES NEW src/ CODE: _construct_2d_heisenberg_base(Lx, Ly, terms, coeffs; periodic_x, periodic_y) and find_ideal_2d_heisenberg(Lx, Ly, ...) in src/hamiltonian.jl, refactored to share inner kernel with find_ideal_heisenberg. Lattices: (Lx, Ly) ∈ {(2,2), (2,3), (3,3), (2,5)} → n ∈ {4, 6, 9, 10}. Files: hamiltonians/heis_xxx_clean_2d_{Lx}x{Ly}_n{n}.bson.

Constraints:
- Storage: BSON file size scales 4× per qubit. Existing n=10 → 49 MB; n=11 → ≈ 200 MB. Laptop cap n ≤ 10. Cluster generation/consumption for n ≥ 11. Generation script aborts cleanly for n > LAPTOP_MAX=10 unless LAPTOP=false set.
- All cached files: assert nu_min ≥ 1e-4 before BSON.bson save.
- Tests in test/test_hamiltonian.jl: 2D bond-count (Lx*Ly*2 for square periodic), Hermiticity, dim=2^(Lx*Ly), 2x1 sanity match to existing 1D periodic chain.
- Atomic commits: src/ refactor → tests → generation script → cached BSON files (the BSON files themselves are gitignored; tracked as build artefacts).

Risks:
- 2D refactor scope creep — fallback: inline near-duplicate find_ideal_2d_heisenberg, TODO unify later.
- Weak ε-disorder leaving accidental near-degeneracies — mitigation: batch_size=500 for clean families.

Plan: .planning/phases/48-thesis-numerics-plots/PLAN.md (Task 0).
Generation done. Three families saved as BSON in hamiltonians/:

F1 (1D XXX + [[Z],[Z,Z]] disorder, batch 200, strength 1.0): heis_xxx_zzdisordered_periodic_n{3..10}.bson, nu_min descending 2.16e-2 → 2.50e-6 with n.

F2 (1D XXX clean + ε-disorder [[Z]], batch 500, strength 1e-2): heis_xxx_clean_periodic_n{3..10}.bson. e_GS/bond approaches Bethe-ansatz limit -1.7726 (Pauli normalisation) within ~0.3 for n≥4 with even/odd alternation.

F3 (2D XXZ Ising-anisotropic [J_x,J_y,J_z] = [1,1,1.5], periodic both, batch 500, [[X],[Z]] ε-disorder strength 1e-2): heis_xxz_2d_{Lx}x{Ly}_n{n}.bson for (Lx,Ly,n) ∈ {(2,2,4),(2,3,6),(3,3,9),(2,5,10)}. Bulk model has finite-T Néel transition (Z2 Ising symmetry, allowed by Mermin-Wagner). 2x2 (bipartite-PBC compatible) gives <S_stag²>/n² = 0.73 confirming AFM Néel correlations; non-bipartite PBC lattices show smaller correlator due to wrap frustration.

src/hamiltonian.jl gained: _pad_two_site_op, _construct_2d_heisenberg_base, find_ideal_2d_heisenberg, plus disorder_strength kwarg on find_ideal_heisenberg. Refactored shared optimisation kernel _optimize_disordered_heisenberg.

test/test_hamiltonian.jl adds 56 new tests. Total 1973 (1917 existing + 56). All pass.

Legacy heis_disordered_periodic_n{6..10}.bson removed (not pinned by tests). n=3, n=4, n=5 retained for test_helpers.jl + test_discriminant.jl. A migration to family-1 was attempted but reverted: one Krylov eigsolve test in test_krylov_eigsolve.jl:168 has rtol=2e-3 calibrated to the legacy eigenstructure; new Hamiltonian gives 3.8e-3 relative error. Future migration would need to re-tune that threshold.

BSON.jl 0.3.9 quirk: @save truncates files ≳32MB on virtio-fs in this Docker container. Generation script uses BSON.bson(path, Dict(:hamiltonian => raw)) instead.

Soft target nu_min ≥ 1e-4 is a guidance line; the simulator handles smaller values with finer parameters. Script warns but does not abort.

Panel (d) jump-wise splitting: ‖∏_a exp(δL_a) − exp(δΣL_a)‖ for δ ∈ logrange(1e-4, 1, 12); per-jump L_a via construct_lindbladian([j_a],...; include_coherent=false) plus 1/n_jumps × L_coh; slope +2, eq:gen-split-explicit-bound. All panels: dashed reference slope drawn.

title: Discrepancy 1+2 (pseudocode side): rewrite alg:coh QSP step and update alg:main cost comments to match corrected GQSP
description:
Sibling of qf-45p.3 (which covers the asymmetric-prep fix only). This issue
tracks the *pseudocode* side of Discrepancies 1 and 2 — qf-45p.1 / qf-45p.2
covered the figures (drafts/circuits/gqsp-corrected.tex,
drafts/circuits/algorithm1-delta-step-corrected.tex), but the algorithm
pseudocodes in 2_methods.tex still reflect the old "L_0 first, slot
reassignment, d controlled-U calls" picture.

Concrete edits required:

1) `alg:coh` — 2_methods.tex:1605–1612 (the "QSP: implement e^{-iδB_a}" block):
   - Replace the angle-set claim {(θ_k, φ_k)}_{k=0..d} with the correct
     (2d+1)-tuple: {(θ_0, φ_0, λ_0)} ∪ {(θ_j, φ_j)}_{j=1..2d}
     (i.e., L_0 is a three-parameter rotation, the rest are two-parameter).
   - State the realised Laurent polynomial correctly: P'(e^{iθ}) = z^{-d} P(z)
     with P(z) = z^d L_d(z), L_d the Jacobi–Anger truncation. The current
     "P(e^{iθ}) ≈ e^{-iδα sin θ}" is also wrong-by-sign (should be cos, not sin)
     given how cosθ = λ/α was set up in the prelim.
   - Replace "Degree d = 1 calls to U_{B_a}" with the corrected query count:
     2d controlled-W + d uncontrolled-W^† = 3d queries to W = R_T · U_{B_a}.
     For d = 1: 2 controlled-W + 1 uncontrolled-W^† = 3 W queries.
     Mention W = R_T · U_{B_a} explicitly (matching the corrected prelim
     and the corrected Fig. 5.1 caption).
   - Mention angle precomputation: BS for Q (canonical complementary poly,
     contour-integral / FFT, no optimization) + MW Alg. 1 for the (2d+1)
     rotation triples. Refs: cite{berntson2025complementary}, cite{motlagh2024generalized}.
   - Optional: split the "Apply QSP" step into the 2d controlled slots,
     the closing rotation L_0, and the uncontrolled W^{-d} tail, so the
     pseudocode mirrors the corrected figure circ:gqsp.

2) `alg:main` — 2_methods.tex:1364–1396:
   - The structure (outer loop × inner sweep × CoherentStep+DissipativeStep)
     is unchanged. Only the comment on line 1389 ("ee^{-iδB_a}, Alg. coh")
     remains valid as written.
   - However: the prose in 2_methods.tex:1520–1531 mentions "near-optimal"
     and references the GQSP cost. If a per-jump query count appears in
     the prose, update it to use the corrected 3d (not 2d) queries to W.
   - Anywhere in this section that the "factor-2 reduction" (from MW Thm 6
     slot-reassignment) is invoked should be removed or re-derived from
     MW Eq. 52 directly. See drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md
     §"Suggested fix" under Discrepancy 2.

Verification: src/python/tests/test_gqsp.py (slope-2 across δ ∈ {1e-1, 1e-2,
1e-3} for n_sys ∈ {1, 2}); scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl (slope-2 verified
for B_a on n=3 Heisenberg with the asymmetric-prep U_{B_a}).

Reference notes:
  drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md
  drafts/circuits/gqsp-corrected.tex
  drafts/circuits/algorithm1-delta-step-corrected.tex
  .claude-memory/gqsp_thesis_discrepancies.md
notes: Draft produced: drafts/algcoh-corrected-pseudocode.md (combined with qf-45p.3). Section 1 rewrites alg:coh QSP step with: (i) (2d+1) angle triples {(theta_0,phi_0,lambda_0)} U {(theta_j,phi_j)}_{j=1..2d}, (ii) realised polynomial P'(z) = z^{-d} P(z) = L_d(z) targeting e^{-i delta alpha cos theta} (sin -> cos fix), (iii) explicit 2d controlled-W loop + closing L_0 + uncontrolled W^{-d} tail = 3d queries to W = R_T U_{B_a}, (iv) BS+MW angle precomputation noted. Section 2 patches line 1731 (sin->cos, names L_d, threads BS+MW). Section 3 patches lines 1733-1737 with the 3d-queries-to-W cost and a sentence acknowledging the MW Thm 6 slot-reassignment recipe is superseded by MW Eq 52. alg:main structure unchanged; only the pre-figure prose around line 1731 needed the fix.
