title: Step 4: end-to-end verification (physics-checker, code-verifier, full test suite)
description:
Final verification pass after Steps 1-3 are merged.

## Sub-tasks

### physics-checker on α choice and parameter regimes

Validate:
- The thesis-faithful α `= γ_nf · t0² · ‖b_-‖_1 · ‖b_+‖_1 · ‖A‖²` correctly bounds `‖B_a‖_op` for the simulator's discretized B_a (i.e., the discrete Riemann sum stays under the L1-norm bound).
- For typical numerics-chapter parameters (n=3, β=1, σ=0.5, gaussian_parameters set), δα for typical δ ∈ {1e-3, 1e-2} is well below 1 — confirming d=1 is in the slope-2 regime.
- The "γ_nf factor on α" is correct: the simulator-side `B_a` is `γ_nf`-scaled, so its block-encoding α must be `γ_nf`-scaled too. Independently re-derive vs the thesis Alg.~ref{alg:coh}.
- Document the regime where d≥2 would be preferable (large δα → degraded convergence at d=1; specific param ranges).

### code-verifier sweep

- Independently confirm the Chebyshev formula `f_d(x) = J_0(δα) + Σ_{k=1}^d 2 (-i)^k J_k(δα) T_k(x)` matches the post-selected anc=|0⟩ block of the GQSP circuit (port the `walk_from_block_encoding + laurent_apply` recipe from `scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl` into a test).
- Slope-(d+1) check on random Hermitian B for d ∈ {1, 2, 3} — confirm asymptotic δ-scaling matches Bessel-tail bound `2 (e δα / (2(d+1)))^(d+1)`.
- `run_thermalize` with `with_gqsp=true` and `with_gqsp=false` converge to the same Gibbs state within O(δ × T_total) trace distance.
- Audit for hot-path allocations regressions vs the matrix-exp baseline.

### Full Julia test suite

- `julia --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.test()'` passes once at the end of the epic.
- Test count: ≥ 1860 (existing) + new tests from qf-63j.1, qf-63j.2, qf-63j.3 — confirm new total and no skipped tests.

### Memory note

Add to `.claude-memory/`:
- `gqsp_simulator_integration.md` — short note: feature added, default off, regime where d=1 suffices, files touched, pointer to `scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl` for the underlying derivation.
- Update `MEMORY.md` index.

## Done when

- physics-checker report attached to this issue (or in `proofs/gqsp/` as a small ledger entry).
- code-verifier sign-off: all checks pass.
- Full test suite passes (≥ 1880+ expected total).
- Memory note added.
- Atomic commit: `chore(memory): document GQSP simulator integration (qf-63j.4)`.
close_reason: Physics-checker confirmed α formula matches triangle bound; opnorm(B_a)/α ~0.05-0.3 for n=3 fixture, δα ≪ 0.1 for typical δ — comfortably in slope-2 regime. Memory note gqsp_simulator_integration.md added documenting the feature, regime analysis, and where d≥2 is preferable. Full test suite passes (1917 tests, 1860+57 new across qf-63j.1-3).

title: Step 3: integrate GQSP into _precompute_coherent_unitary and trajectory workspace
description:
Wire `_gqsp_apply_polynomial` into the two coherent-unitary precomputation sites. Reuse `B_time`/`B_trotter`/`_precompute_coherent_B` — only the line that builds `U_a` from `B_a` changes.

## Files

### `src/coherent.jl` — `_precompute_coherent_unitary`

Existing structure (lines 73-99):
```julia
if config.domain isa TimeDomain
    (; b_minus, b_plus, gamma_norm_factor) = precomputed_data
    @inbounds for (k, jump) in pairs(jumps)
        B = B_time([jump], hamiltonian, b_minus, b_plus, config.t0, config.beta, config.sigma)
        rmul!(B, gamma_norm_factor)
        U_terms[k] = exp(-1im * delta * Hermitian(B))
    end
elseif config.domain isa TrotterDomain
    (; b_minus, b_plus, gamma_norm_factor) = precomputed_data
    @assert trotter !== nothing
    @inbounds for (k, jump) in pairs(jumps)
        B = B_trotter([jump], trotter, b_minus, b_plus, config.beta, config.sigma)
        rmul!(B, gamma_norm_factor)
        U_terms[k] = exp(-1im * delta * Hermitian(B))
    end
else
    # BohrDomain / EnergyDomain — unchanged
    ...
end
```

Change pattern: in the `TimeDomain` and `TrotterDomain` branches, dispatch on `config.with_gqsp`. Outside the loop (cheap), precompute the L1 sums and any shared scratch needed for Clenshaw if d ≥ 2:
```julia
if config.domain isa TimeDomain
    (; b_minus, b_plus, gamma_norm_factor) = precomputed_data
    if config.with_gqsp
        l1_minus = sum(abs, values(b_minus))
        l1_plus  = sum(abs, values(b_plus))
        # Optional: pre-allocate Clenshaw scratch if config.gqsp_degree ≥ 2 — share across jumps
        @inbounds for (k, jump) in pairs(jumps)
            B = B_time([jump], hamiltonian, b_minus, b_plus, config.t0, config.beta, config.sigma)
            rmul!(B, gamma_norm_factor)
            α_k = gamma_norm_factor * config.t0^2 * l1_minus * l1_plus * opnorm(jump.data)^2
            U_terms[k] = _gqsp_apply_polynomial(B, α_k, delta, config.gqsp_degree)
        end
    else
        @inbounds for (k, jump) in pairs(jumps)
            B = B_time([jump], hamiltonian, b_minus, b_plus, config.t0, config.beta, config.sigma)
            rmul!(B, gamma_norm_factor)
            U_terms[k] = exp(-1im * delta * Hermitian(B))
        end
    end
elseif config.domain isa TrotterDomain
    # mirror the TimeDomain branch but with trotter.t0 and B_trotter
    ...
end
```

If `_gqsp_apply_polynomial` factors are kept as a single function call, the duplication between the two branches is just the `B_time` vs `B_trotter` and `config.t0` vs `trotter.t0` — same as today. Acceptable. If a small helper `_build_one_coherent_unitary(B, jump, ...)` cleans this up, write it.

### `src/trajectories.jl` — workspace init (around lines 92-99)

Existing:
```julia
per_op_U_B = Vector{Union{Nothing, Matrix{CT}}}(undef, n_jumps)
if with_coherent(config.construction)
    @inbounds for a in 1:n_jumps
        single_jump = JumpOp[jumps[a]]
        B_a = _precompute_coherent_B(single_jump, ham_or_trott, config, precomputed_data)
        hermitianize!(B_a)
        per_op_U_B[a] = exp(-1im * (delta / p_jump) * Hermitian(B_a))
    end
else
    fill!(per_op_U_B, nothing)
end
```

Change to:
- If `config.with_gqsp` and `with_coherent(config.construction)`: assert `config.domain isa Union{TimeDomain, TrotterDomain}` (validation already guards this; assertion is defensive). Compute α_a and call `_gqsp_apply_polynomial(B_a, α_a, delta/p_jump, config.gqsp_degree)`.
- Else (existing path): `exp(-1im * (delta/p_jump) * Hermitian(B_a))`.

For TrotterDomain in trajectories, t0_sim = trotter.t0 (`ham_or_trott` is `TrottTrott` — read `.t0`); for TimeDomain, t0_sim = config.t0. Confirm `precomputed_data.b_minus`/`b_plus` are populated in both cases (they are — see `furnace_utensils.jl` `_precompute_data` for TimeDomain/TrotterDomain).

## Tests (new file `test/test_gqsp_thermalize.jl`)

### Smoke test
- Build a small (n=3 or n=4) Heisenberg system, KMS, TimeDomain, with_gqsp=true, gqsp_degree=1.
- Run `run_thermalize` for a small number of steps. Assert: no errors, finite trace distance, returns valid `ThermalizeResults`.
- Same for TrotterDomain.

### Regression vs matrix-exp baseline
- Same setup as smoke test, run with `with_gqsp=true` and `with_gqsp=false` to the *same final time*.
- Use small δ (e.g. 1e-3) so the O(δ²) GQSP error is dominated by other errors and trace distances should match closely (e.g., within 1e-4 absolute).
- Assert the two evolutions converge to the same Gibbs state (within a relaxed tolerance for the GQSP one).

### Larger δ — verify error scales
- Run with δ ∈ {1e-1, 1e-2, 1e-3} for fixed total time.
- Compute `‖final_dm_gqsp - final_dm_baseline‖`. Verify this is bounded by O(δ²) per step × n_steps (i.e., O(δ × T_total)).

### Trajectory path
- Same regression test for `run_trajectory`. Single trajectory, fixed seed; check the final density matrix matches the matrix-exp baseline within O(δ²) tolerance.

### Allocation regression
- Use `@allocated` on a single hot-loop step via the trajectory `step_along_trajectory!` path. Verify GQSP path doesn't allocate more than the matrix-exp path (both should hit the precomputed `U_a` via `mul!`).

## Done when

- All new tests pass.
- Existing 1860+ tests still pass.
- `_precompute_coherent_unitary` and trajectory workspace cleanly dispatch on `config.with_gqsp` without duplicating `B_time`/`B_trotter`/`_precompute_coherent_B` calls.
- Atomic commits:
  - `feat(coherent): integrate GQSP into _precompute_coherent_unitary (qf-63j.3a)`
  - `feat(trajectories): integrate GQSP into trajectory workspace init (qf-63j.3b)`
  - `test(gqsp): regression and smoke tests for GQSP coherent step (qf-63j.3c)`

## Notes for code-integrator

- The existing pattern keeps `B = ...; rmul!(B, gnf); U = ...` inline rather than factoring into a helper. Match this — DO NOT introduce a new "build coherent unitary" abstraction that hides the dispatch. Inline `if config.with_gqsp` branch is fine and matches the codebase's tendency to dispatch on Config fields inline.
- Reuse: `B_time`, `B_trotter`, `_precompute_coherent_B`, `precomputed_data.b_minus`/`b_plus`/`gamma_norm_factor`, `config.t0`, `trotter.t0`. NO new imports beyond `besselj`.
- Notation: keep variable names consistent with the file (e.g., `B`, `U_terms`, `gamma_norm_factor`). The α here is "block-encoding alpha" — name it `alpha_be` or `α_be` to disambiguate from the CPTP `alpha = 1 - sqrt(1-delta)` already used in `_build_cptp_channel`.
close_reason: _precompute_coherent_unitary and _build_trajectory_workspace dispatch on config.with_gqsp; α_a computed via thesis block-encoding norm. 16 regression/smoke tests cover Time+TrotterDomain run_thermalize and run_trajectory. Test count 1901→1917. Three atomic commits: 4c7438c (coherent), adaf4fe (trajectories), 154b13b (tests).

title: Step 2: implement Jacobi-Anger Chebyshev polynomial and α helper in coherent.jl
description:
Standalone, well-typed implementations in `src/coherent.jl` matching existing style (preallocation, parametric `T`, no `Any` in hot path, Clenshaw recurrence). No integration yet — that's Step 3.

## New functions (in `src/coherent.jl`)

### `_gqsp_block_encoding_alpha(jump, b_minus, b_plus, t0_sim, gamma_norm_factor) -> Real`

Compute the simulator block-encoding norm:
```
α_a = gamma_norm_factor · t0_sim² · (Σ_t |b_-(t)|) · (Σ_τ |b_+(τ)|) · ‖A_a‖²_op
```

Reads `b_minus`, `b_plus` as `Dict` (the existing truncated-func format from `_compute_truncated_func`). `‖A_a‖²` via `opnorm(jump.data)^2`. Returns a `Float64` (or `T` matching config).

### `_gqsp_apply_polynomial(B::AbstractMatrix{<:Complex}, α::Real, delta::Real, d::Int) -> Matrix{<:Complex}`

Compute `f_d(B/α) = J_0(δα) I + Σ_{k=1}^{d} 2 (-i)^k J_k(δα) T_k(B/α)` where `T_k` is the Chebyshev polynomial of the first kind. Implementation:

- Coefficients: `a_0 = besselj(0, δα)`, `a_k = 2 cis(-π k / 2) besselj(k, δα)` for `k ≥ 1`.
- **d=1 fast path** (default, no matmul): `result = a_0 I + a_1 (B/α)` via scalar-axpy on a fresh allocation.
- **d≥2 Clenshaw recurrence on Chebyshev T_k(x), x = B/α**:
  ```
  u_{d+1} = u_{d+2} = 0  (n×n zero matrices)
  for j = d, d-1, ..., 1:
      u_j = 2 (B/α) u_{j+1} - u_{j+2} + a_j I
  result = a_0 I + (B/α) u_1 - u_2
  ```
  Uses three n×n scratch buffers (rotated each iteration), so allocation is O(d) → O(1) since buffers are reused.
- Return `result` as `Matrix{CT}` (concrete type). Hermiticity is *not* preserved in general (f_d is a unitary modulo O((δα)^{d+1})); do not Hermitianize the output.

### Bessel function: `besselj`

Already available via `using SpecialFunctions: besselj` (note: existing `using SpecialFunctions: erfc` in `QuantumFurnace.jl` line 14; extend the import to add `besselj`).

## Tests (new file `test/test_gqsp_polynomial.jl`)

- **d=1 closed form**: For random Hermitian B with `‖B‖ ≤ α`, verify `_gqsp_apply_polynomial(B, α, δ, 1) ≈ J_0(δα) I - 2i J_1(δα) B/α` to machine precision.
- **d=2 closed form**: verify `_gqsp_apply_polynomial(B, α, δ, 2) ≈ (J_0+2J_2) I - 2i J_1 (B/α) - 4 J_2 (B/α)²` to machine precision.
- **Slope (d+1) verification**: For random Hermitian B with `‖B‖ = α = 1`, sweep δ ∈ {1e-1, 5e-2, 1e-2}, compute `‖f_d(B/α) - exp(-iδB)‖_op`. For d=1, slope ≈ 2; for d=2, slope ≈ 3; for d=3, slope ≈ 4.
- **α scaling**: For fixed δ, doubling α should double δα → expect 2^(d+1) increase in error (for d=1, 4× error). Verify on small example.
- **Reduces to identity at δ=0**: `_gqsp_apply_polynomial(B, α, 0.0, d) ≈ I` for any d, α (since J_0(0)=1, J_k(0)=0 for k≥1).
- **Block encoding alpha helper**: For a synthetic precomputed_data with known b_minus, b_plus dicts, verify `_gqsp_block_encoding_alpha` returns the expected product. Use `opnorm` on a Pauli (=1).
- **Verification against scratch script POC**: port the n=3 Heisenberg setup from `scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl` and confirm `_gqsp_apply_polynomial(B_a, α, δ, 1)` matches `[L_d(W)]_{anc=|0⟩}` from the scratch script's `walk_from_block_encoding` + `laurent_apply` pipeline (this is the operator-level vs circuit-level equivalence).

## Done when

- All new functions exported (or kept internal — pick the convention; `B_time` is exported, `_compute_b_minus` is not, suggesting underscore-private is fine for these helpers; `_gqsp_apply_polynomial` is internal).
- New tests pass; existing 1860+ tests still pass.
- Atomic commit: `feat(coherent): GQSP polynomial approximation function + alpha helper (qf-63j.2)`.

## Notes for sci-coder

- Use `besselj` from `SpecialFunctions` (already a dep — see line 14 of `src/QuantumFurnace.jl`).
- Use `cis(-π k / 2)` for `(-i)^k` (matches `scratch_gqsp_poly.jl`).
- Reuse Clenshaw scratch buffers across multiple jumps (the integration step in qf-63j.3 will allocate them once outside the per-jump loop).
- Match existing `coherent.jl` patterns: `CT = Complex{eltype(...)}`, `@inbounds` on hot loops, `mul!(out, A, B, alpha, beta)` for fused multiply-add.
close_reason: Implemented _gqsp_apply_polynomial (d=1 fast-path scalar-axpy; d≥2 Clenshaw on T_k(B/α) with 3 reused scratch buffers) and _gqsp_block_encoding_alpha (γ_nf · t0² · ‖b_-‖_1 · ‖b_+‖_1 · ‖A‖²). 33 new tests cover closed forms, slope-(d+1), joint (B,α,δ)-invariance, α-doubling error ratio, operator/circuit equivalence vs 1-ancilla qubitization, and n=3 Heisenberg slope-2 anchor. Test count 1868→1901.

title: Step 1: add with_gqsp/gqsp_degree fields to Config + validate_config!
description:
Schema-level change in `src/structs.jl` and validation update in `src/misc_tools.jl`.

## Files

- `src/structs.jl` — add to the `@kwdef struct Config{S, D, C, T}`:
  - `with_gqsp::Bool = false` (place under "Thermalize-specific" or in a new "GQSP" comment block since both Thermalize and Trajectory use it).
  - `gqsp_degree::Int = 1` (default d=1, matching the regime δα ≪ 1 where the O((δα)²) error matches the splitting error).
- `src/misc_tools.jl` — extend `validate_config!`:
  - If `config.with_gqsp == true`:
    - require `with_coherent(config.construction) == true` (else: "with_gqsp requires a construction with coherent term (KMS or DLL)")
    - require `config.domain isa Union{TimeDomain, TrotterDomain}` (else: "with_gqsp is only supported for TimeDomain or TrotterDomain")
    - require `config.gqsp_degree ≥ 1` (else: "gqsp_degree must be ≥ 1")
    - require `config.gqsp_degree ≤ 100` (matching the GQSP epic's hard cap)

## Tests (new test_*.jl or extension to existing)

- Default `with_gqsp=false` and `gqsp_degree=1` on a sample Config.
- `validate_config!` passes for valid `with_gqsp=true, KMS, TimeDomain, gqsp_degree=1`.
- `validate_config!` errors for `with_gqsp=true, GNS, TimeDomain` (no coherent term).
- `validate_config!` errors for `with_gqsp=true, KMS, EnergyDomain`.
- `validate_config!` errors for `with_gqsp=true, KMS, TimeDomain, gqsp_degree=0`.
- `validate_config!` errors for `with_gqsp=true, KMS, TimeDomain, gqsp_degree=101`.

## Done when

- All existing 1860+ tests still pass.
- New validation tests pass.
- Atomic commit: `feat(config): add with_gqsp/gqsp_degree fields and validation (qf-63j.1)`.
close_reason: Added with_gqsp::Bool=false and gqsp_degree::Int=1 fields to Config; validate_config! rejects with_gqsp without coherent term, outside Time/TrotterDomain, or gqsp_degree outside [1,100]. 8 new tests in test/test_gqsp_config.jl. Full suite 1868 tests pass.

title: GQSP coherent step in Julia Thermalize simulator (with_gqsp option for Time/TrotterDomain)
description:
# Add GQSP polynomial approximation for the coherent step in the Julia simulator

## Goal

Match the Julia Thermalize/Trajectory simulator with the **thesis algorithm as written**: generator splitting both jump-wise and coherent-dissipative-wise, where the **coherent** part is realised by the GQSP polynomial approximation (Alg.~ref{alg:coh}) and the **dissipative** part by the Chen et al. weak-measurement CPTP channel.

Currently, the simulator's coherent step uses an **exact matrix exponential** `U_a = exp(-i δ B_a)` (per jump, precomputed). The thesis prescribes a **degree-d polynomial approximation** of `exp(-i δ B_a)` via GQSP, which is faithful to O(δ²) for d=1.

This epic introduces a `with_gqsp::Bool` flag (default `false`) on `Config` so the simulator can be run in either mode, with `with_gqsp=true` requiring `with_coherent(construction)=true` and `domain ∈ {TimeDomain, TrotterDomain}`.

## Background — what was verified previously

Epic `qf-0x6` (closed) verified the GQSP pipeline end-to-end at the **circuit level**:

- Jacobi-Anger Laurent polynomial `L_d(e^{iθ}) = Σ_{k=-d}^{d} (-i)^k J_k(δα) e^{ikθ}` for `e^{-iδα cos θ}` — `scripts/scratch_gqsp_poly.jl`.
- Berntson-Sünderhauf complementary polynomial Q + Motlagh-Wiebe Algorithm 1 angle extraction — `scripts/scratch_gqsp_random_h.jl`.
- Qiskit POC matching slope-2 across n_sys ∈ {1, 2} — `src/python/gqsp/`, `src/python/tests/test_gqsp.py`.
- Thesis-style block encoding `U_{B_a}` for n=3 Heisenberg with asymmetric `PREP'_±/PREP_±^†` — `scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl`. Block-encoding identity ‖⟨0|U_{B_a}|0⟩ − B_a/α‖ ≈ 1.75e-16 at machine precision (for fully-decayed kernels).
- End-to-end MW Eq 52 form: `[L_d(W)]_{anc=|0⟩}` matches `exp(-iδ B_a)` with slope 2.

Three thesis discrepancies were surfaced and tracked under `qf-45p` (drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md). **The thesis text has not yet been updated.** This epic implements the algorithm as it *should be* written (matching the verified POC), not as the prelim/methods chapter currently reads.

## Key insight — operator-level equivalent of the GQSP circuit

The post-selected anc=|0⟩ block of the GQSP circuit `(I⊗W^{-d}) · ∏_j R_j · A_j · L_0` (MW Eq 52 form) acting on the qubitization walk `W = R_anc · U_{B_a}` is, by qubitization + Jacobi-Anger:

$$
[L_d(W)]_{\rm anc=|0\rangle} \;=\; f_d(B_a/\alpha) \;:=\; J_0(\delta\alpha)\,I + \sum_{k=1}^{d} 2\,(-i)^k\,J_k(\delta\alpha)\,T_k(B_a/\alpha)
$$

with `T_k` the Chebyshev polynomial of degree k. Derivation (verified):
- Pair k with −k in `L_d(e^{iθ})` using `J_{-k} = (-1)^k J_k`.
- Recognise `cos(kθ) = T_k(cos θ) = T_k(λ/α)` per spectral component.
- Imaginary cross-terms (Chebyshev U_k pieces) live entirely in the off-diagonal anc=|0⟩→|1⟩ block — the post-selected block is purely the T_k expansion above.

For d=1: `f_1(x) = J_0(δα) - 2i J_1(δα) x`.
For d=2: `f_2(x) = (J_0 + 2J_2) - 2i J_1 x - 4 J_2 x²`.

**This is what the simulator computes, applied as a matrix function of `B_a` with `α_a = ‖B_a‖`-bounding scalar.** No need to build the full block-encoding circuit at the simulator level — `f_d(B_a/α_a)` IS the operator the GQSP circuit produces on the post-selected block, to O((δα)^{d+1}).

## α choice — thesis-faithful block encoding norm

Following Alg.~ref{alg:coh}: `α_a = ‖b_-‖_1 · ‖b_+‖_1 · ‖A_a‖²` (continuous), discretized as

$$
\alpha_a^{\rm sim} \;=\; \gamma_{\rm nf} \cdot t_0^2 \cdot \Big(\sum_t |b_-(t)|\Big) \cdot \Big(\sum_\tau |b_+(\tau)|\Big) \cdot \|A_a\|_{\rm op}^2
$$

where the `γ_nf` factor comes from the fact that B_a in the simulator is `gamma_norm_factor`-scaled (see `_precompute_coherent_B`). This guarantees `‖B_a/α_a‖_op ≤ 1` and makes the simulator faithful to the thesis algorithm.

`b_minus`, `b_plus` dicts and `gamma_norm_factor` are already in `precomputed_data` (see `src/furnace_utensils.jl` `_precompute_data`). `t_0` is `config.t0` (TimeDomain) or `trotter.t0` (TrotterDomain). `‖A_a‖²` is `opnorm(jump.data)^2`.

## Allocation analysis

- Matrix-exp path (current): per-jump `Vector{Matrix{CT}}` of length `n_jumps`, each `dim × dim`. Construction needs eigendecomposition of B_a + matrix exponential (most expensive part).
- GQSP d=1 path: SAME per-jump cache slot, SAME storage. Construction: `J_0(δα) I - 2i J_1(δα) B_a/α_a` — purely scalar-matrix axpy, no matmul, no eigendecomposition. **Cheaper.**
- GQSP d=2 path: one extra matmul (B_a²) at construction time, three dim² scratch buffers shared across jumps. Hot path is identical.
- GQSP d≥3 path: Clenshaw recurrence — d-1 matmuls at construction, three dim² scratch buffers (u_j, u_{j+1}, u_{j+2}) reused across jumps. Hot path still identical.

Conclusion: allocation pattern is unchanged at the simulator level. Storing the precomputed `U_a` matrix (rather than coefficients + B_a separately) is correct: it amortises construction over thousands of hot-loop steps.

## Children (to be created as separate tasks)

1. **Add `with_gqsp` and `gqsp_degree` to `Config`; validation.** Type: schema. Files: `src/structs.jl`, `src/misc_tools.jl`, tests for `validate_config!`.
2. **Implement Jacobi-Anger Chebyshev polynomial `_gqsp_apply_polynomial(B, α, δ, d)` in `src/coherent.jl`.** Also `_gqsp_block_encoding_alpha`. Unit tests: d=1 closed form vs `J_0(δα) I - 2i J_1(δα) B/α`; d=2,3 vs Chebyshev sum; ‖f_d − exp(-iδB)‖ slope (d+1) on random Hermitian B; Hermiticity-preserving structure.
3. **Integrate into `_precompute_coherent_unitary` (Thermalize DM) and trajectory workspace init.** Files: `src/coherent.jl` (dispatch on `config.with_gqsp` in TimeDomain/TrotterDomain branches), `src/trajectories.jl` (lines 92-99). Regression: `run_thermalize`/`run_trajectory` with `with_gqsp=true, d=1` matches `with_gqsp=false` baseline up to the documented O(δ²) approximation tolerance for small δ. No allocation regressions vs baseline.
4. **End-to-end verification: physics-checker on α choice, code-verifier sweep, full Julia test suite once at the end.** Sanity-check that for typical thesis params, `δα ≪ 1` so d=1 truncation is well within the slope-2 regime. Confirm `1860+` test count after the new module-level tests.

## Agent assignments

- **`sci-coder`** writes `_gqsp_apply_polynomial` and `_gqsp_block_encoding_alpha` as standalone, well-typed functions matching existing `coherent.jl` conventions (preallocation, parametric `T`, no `Any`, Clenshaw recurrence using shared scratch buffers).
- **`code-integrator`** integrates with the existing `_precompute_coherent_unitary` dispatch and the `trajectories.jl` parallel branch — reuses `B_time`/`B_trotter`, the `precomputed_data` named tuple, the per-jump `U_terms` vector, and the same in-place patterns. No "hacky" duplication; if the matrix-exp/GQSP branches share enough structure, factor a small helper. Adjustments to existing functions only if motivated.
- **`code-verifier`** verifies (a) the Chebyshev formula matches the post-selected anc=|0⟩ block of the GQSP circuit on a small example (port from `scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl`); (b) `f_d(B/α) → exp(-iδB)` with slope `(d+1)` on random Hermitian B; (c) `run_thermalize` regression vs matrix-exp baseline; (d) full test suite passes (≥1860 tests).
- **`physics-checker`** sanity-checks the α choice against the thesis (Alg.~ref{alg:coh}, eq:B-block-encoding) and confirms the regime `δα < 1` is naturally satisfied for typical numerics-chapter parameters.

## Constraints

- **`with_gqsp=false` is the default** — no behavioural change for existing scripts/tests.
- Validation: `with_gqsp=true` must throw a clear error if `with_coherent(construction)=false` or if `domain ∉ {TimeDomain, TrotterDomain}` or if `gqsp_degree < 1`.
- Hard cap `gqsp_degree ≤ 100` (matching the GQSP epic's hard cap).
- Reuse existing structs (`Config`, `JumpOp`, `HamHam`, `TrottTrott`, `ThermalizeScratch`) and existing helpers (`B_time`, `B_trotter`, `_precompute_coherent_B`, `_compute_truncated_func`).
- Atomic commits per child (one logical change each).
- Tests must pass after each commit.

## Closure criteria

- All four child tasks closed.
- Regression test: `run_thermalize` with `with_gqsp=true, gqsp_degree=1` matches `with_gqsp=false` to within the documented O(δ²) tolerance for δ ≤ 1e-2 on a small (n=3 or n=4) Heisenberg test setup, on both TimeDomain and TrotterDomain.
- Full Julia test suite passes once at the end of the epic.
- Memory note added summarising the new feature + key parameter ranges.

## Source-of-truth references

- `drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md` — discrepancies between thesis and verified POC (the simulator implements the verified POC, not the still-incorrect thesis).
- `scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl` — verified construction of `U_{B_a}`, walk W, and `[L_d(W)]_{anc=|0⟩}` for n=3 Heisenberg. Numerical anchor for code-verifier.
- `scripts/scratch_gqsp_random_h.jl` — Jacobi-Anger + BS+MW + slope-2 verification on random H.
- `src/coherent.jl` — existing `_precompute_coherent_unitary`, `B_time`, `B_trotter` (the integration target).
- `src/trajectories.jl` lines 92-99 — parallel matrix-exp branch (the second integration target).
- `src/structs.jl` — `Config` definition (where the new fields go).
- Thesis: 2_methods.tex Alg.~ref{alg:coh}, eq:B-block-encoding, Fig.~ref{circ:U_B_a}; 1_preliminaries.tex circ:gqsp.
close_reason: Epic complete: with_gqsp option fully integrated. Schema (qf-63j.1, 822117b) + polynomial (qf-63j.2, 278f600) + integration (qf-63j.3, 4c7438c+adaf4fe+154b13b) + verification (qf-63j.4). 1917 tests pass.

title: Discrepancy 3: rewrite Alg.~ref{alg:coh} to use asymmetric prep PREP'/PREP^† (matches Fig. circ:U_B_a)
description:
Source: drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md (Discrepancy 3).

## Problem

2_methods.tex Alg.~ref{alg:coh} (lines 1556-1597) describes the StatePrep on T_- and T_+ as a single rotation:

    StatePrep(T_-): |0⟩ → |b_-⟩ = Σ_t̄ √(t_0 |b_-(t̄)|/α_-) sgn(b_-(t̄)) |t̄⟩

and the unprep step as StatePrep^†(T_-): |b_-⟩ → |0⟩.

Symmetric application — StatePrep on the forward pass, StatePrep^† on the reverse — projects the inner controlled stuff against ⟨b_-|b_-⟩ which contains |sgn(b_-)|² = 1 at each grid point. This *drops the sign information* of b_-(t) in the block-encoding sum.

Specifically, the block-encoding identity ⟨0_{T_-}⟨0_{T_+}| U_{B_a} |0_{T_-}⟩|0_{T_+}⟩ = B_a/α requires the prep and unprep amplitudes to multiply to b_±(t̄) · t_0 / α_±, not |b_±(t̄)| · t_0 / α_±.

## What is correct (and is already in the figure)

Fig. circ:U_B_a (lines 1620-1696) shows two distinct prep operators:

- **PREP'_±** (signed): |0⟩ → Σ_t̄ b_±(t̄)/√|b_±(t̄)| · √t_0/√α_± |t̄⟩ — used on the forward pass.
- **PREP_±** (magnitude only): |0⟩ → Σ_t̄ √(t_0 |b_±(t̄)|/α_±) |t̄⟩ — its dagger is used on the reverse pass.

The product of forward-prep amplitude × reverse-unprep-amplitude gives b_±(t̄) · t_0 / α_±, as required.

## What to fix

Rewrite Alg. alg:coh lines 1556-1597 to use PREP'_± on the forward pass (steps 2 and 4 of the algorithm) and PREP_±^† on the reverse pass (steps 10 and 12). Match the figure's notation. The figure caption is already correct; the algorithm body needs to mirror it.

## Verified numerically

scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl builds U_{B_a} with the asymmetric prep for n=3 Heisenberg and obtains
    ‖⟨0|U_{B_a}|0⟩ − B_a/α‖ ≈ 7.5e-9
(relative 5e-7; residual is grid-asymmetry, not prep choice). Symmetric prep would give a structurally wrong block encoding (proportional to |B_a| via cross-grid magnitudes, not B_a).

## Done when

- drafts/<slug>.md ready with the rewritten Alg. alg:coh body (Markdown with inline math, per thesis-writing convention).
- Steps 2, 4, 10, 12 of the algorithm explicitly distinguish PREP'_± from PREP_±^†.
- Cross-reference: scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl (the asymmetric-prep construction in build_U_Ba).
notes: Draft produced: drafts/algcoh-corrected-pseudocode.md (combined with qf-45p.4). Section 1 contains the rewritten alg:coh body with Step 4 spelling out the asymmetric prep pair PREP'_- / PREP_-^dagger and PREP'_+ / PREP_+^dagger inside U_{B_a}, matching Fig. circ:U_B_a caption. Verified by scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl (block-encoding error 7.5e-9 for n=3 Heisenberg).

title: Discrepancy 2: replace slot-reassignment recipe with MW Eq 52 form (all-W + uncontrolled W^{-d})
description:
Source: drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md (Discrepancy 2).

## Problem

Prelim text (1_preliminaries.tex lines ~1224-1228, ~1246-1250) describes realising L_d(z) = z^{-d} P(z) via a circuit with d controlled-W slots followed by d controlled-W† slots, asserting that the rotation angles depend on P only (i.e. same angles work for any slot pattern). The cited MW Thm 6 is *claimed* to provide this.

## Why it's wrong

MW Thm 6's proof rests on Eq 48:

    A' = (I ⊗ U†) A

where A = |0⟩⟨0|⊗I + |1⟩⟨1|⊗U and A' = |0⟩⟨0|⊗I + |1⟩⟨1|⊗U†. Direct calculation gives (I ⊗ U†) A = |0⟩⟨0|⊗U† + |1⟩⟨1|⊗I — this is U† on the *opposite* QSP branch from where A' has it. So the commutation argument that pulls (I ⊗ U^{-k}) out of the GQSP product (MW Eqs 49-52) does not actually go through using A'. Verified empirically for N=1, k=1 in both control conventions: top-left of R_1 A† L_0 has constant and z^{-1} coefficients *swapped* relative to z^{-1} P(z).

## What is correct

MW Eq 52 itself, taken as the definition of the realised circuit:

    (I ⊗ U^{-k}) · [∏_{j=1}^d R(θ_j, φ_j, 0) A] · R(θ_0, φ_0, λ)

= all-controlled-W GQSP layers, followed by an *uncontrolled* W^{-k} on (anc, sys). This is what src/python/gqsp/circuit.py:gqsp_circuit(realise='L_d') implements and what gives slope-2 in the POC.

## What to fix

Rewrite the paragraph around eq:qsp-poly (lines ~1224-1228) and the d=k application (lines ~1246-1250) of 1_preliminaries.tex to describe the circuit in MW Eq 52 form:

1. Build all-W controlled-GQSP layers with MW angles → realises P(W) on QSP=|0⟩.
2. Append uncontrolled W^{-d} on (anc, sys) → multiplies to L_d(W) = W^{-d} P(W).

Keep the citation to MW Thm 6 for the *result* (the realisable Laurent polynomial z^{-k}P exists), but drop the slot-reassignment recipe.

Also: update circ:gqsp itself to show the uncontrolled W^{-d} block at the end instead of d controlled-W† slots interleaved with rotations.

## Cost-analysis impact

The 'factor-2 reduction in queries to U vs U†' argument cited from MW (Berry et al. result) was based on the slot-reassignment giving 2d *controlled* queries total (d each of W and W†). With the corrected Eq 52 form, the count is 2d controlled-W + d uncontrolled-W† = 3d queries. The cost section needs to be redone — verify whether the asymptotic claim (d = O(δα + log(1/ε)/loglog(1/ε))) still holds at the constants, and revisit whether the comparison to LCU/QSVT alternatives is still favourable.

## Done when

- drafts/<slug>.md ready with the new prelim paragraph + revised circ:gqsp figure (quantikz2 source).
- Cost-analysis numbers (queries to W, queries to W†, total walk operations) recomputed.
- Cross-reference: src/python/gqsp/circuit.py:gqsp_circuit (the verified Eq 52 implementation), src/python/tests/test_gqsp.py.
notes: circuit drawn: drafts/circuits/gqsp-corrected.tex (all 2d slots Λ(W), uncontrolled W^{-d} added before PREP^†; total cost 3d queries to W)

title: Discrepancy 1: redraw circ:gqsp with L_0 last in time (or annotate the equivalent transposed convention)
description:
Source: drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md (Discrepancy 1).

## Problem

Prelim Fig. circ:gqsp (1_preliminaries.tex lines ~1100-1200) draws L_0 = R(θ_0, φ_0, λ_0) as the *first* gate, with the layers ctrl-W, R_j applied after. But the matrix factorization underlying MW Algorithm 1 / BS Eq (1.2) is

    [P  Q; *  *] = L_0 · ∏_{j=1}^{2d} D(z) R_j

which, read as a circuit (left = applied last), puts L_0 *last*, after R_{2d}, ctrl-W, …, R_1, ctrl-W. With the angles MW Alg 1 produces, only this latter time order yields P(W) on the QSP=|0⟩ block — verified numerically (src/python/tests/test_gqsp.py).

## What to fix

Two acceptable resolutions:

(a) **Redraw circ:gqsp** with L_0 on the right of the figure (last in time) and R_1, …, R_{2d} reading from right to left.

(b) **Keep the visual L_0-first ordering** but state explicitly that the cited factorization is the *transposed* form (BS Eq 1.2 = transpose of MW Eq 7 per BS Footnote 1), and the visual circuit corresponds to the post-selection ⟨0|·|0⟩ of the transpose. This requires an extra sentence and is cosmetically less clean.

I recommend (a). The figure is the canonical reference for the rest of the thesis; getting it right at the source avoids confusion downstream.

## Done when

- drafts/<slug>.md ready with the redrawn figure (quantikz2 source) and a note pinning the convention.
- Updated text around the figure (lines ~1224-1228 of 1_preliminaries.tex) is consistent.
- Cross-reference: src/python/tests/test_gqsp.py (test_qiskit_slope2) confirms which order is correct.

## Out of scope

Discrepancy 2 (slot-reassignment recipe) and Discrepancy 3 (prep asymmetry) are separate child tasks.
notes:
circuit drawn: drafts/circuits/gqsp-corrected.tex (L_0 placed last in time, dashed slot draws (R_j, Λ(W)) with j running 2d→1 left-to-right)
Resolution direction reversed by qf-45p.5 (MW-native, L_0 first). Close this when qf-45p.5 lands; the BS-form circuit drafts/circuits/gqsp-corrected.tex will be replaced rather than kept.
close_reason: Superseded by qf-45p.5 (MW-native ordering matching MW Eq. 42 directly). qf-45p.1 chose the BS-form direction (L_0 last in time, j running 2d→1 left-to-right); qf-45p.5 reverses that and adopts L_0 first in time, j increasing left-to-right (= forward in time), matching the Motlagh-Wiebe paper's literal Eq. 42 factorisation without transposes. Both directions are mathematically equivalent (M_MW = M_BS^T), but MW-native is now the implementation target. The redrawn drafts/circuits/gqsp-corrected.tex from this issue has been rewritten in the qf-45p.5 patch.

title: Thesis revision: GQSP circuit discrepancies (prelim + methods)
description:
Apply the three concrete fixes identified by the GQSP epic (qf-0x6) POC. Source-of-truth: drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md.

## Background

Building a verified Qiskit POC of the GQSP coherent step (epic qf-0x6) surfaced three discrepancies between the thesis description and what actually realises e^{-i δ B_a} via GQSP. Numerical verification: src/python/tests/test_gqsp.py (slope-2 across n_sys ∈ {1,2}), scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl (slope-2 with the thesis nested-LCU U_{B_a} for n=3), 1860 Julia tests pass. Full write-up of each fix in drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md.

## Scope

This is a thesis-revision epic — touches 1_preliminaries.tex (sec:prelim-QSP, Fig. circ:gqsp) and 2_methods.tex (Alg. alg:coh, Fig. circ:U_B_a). The .tex files are read-only per project conventions; each fix produces a Markdown draft in drafts/ that the user copies into the canonical thesis project by hand.

## Closure criteria

- Three drafts produced (one per fix), each ready to paste into the thesis.
- Drafts mention MW Eq 48 / Eq 52 (Discrepancy 2), MW Thm 6 phrasing (Discrepancy 1), and prep asymmetry (Discrepancy 3).
- Cost analysis for the QSP section is recomputed (factor-2 reduction discussion) since Discrepancy 2's recipe change affects gate counts.
- Cross-reference back to the verified POC code: src/python/gqsp/, scripts/scratch_gqsp_*.jl.
notes:
circuits drawn for prelim and Fig 5.1: drafts/circuits/gqsp-corrected.tex (3 rows: QSP, PREP block, S; W as single box; 2d ctrl-W + 1 uncontrolled W^{-d} + L_0 last) and drafts/circuits/algorithm1-delta-step-corrected.tex (4 rows incl. dissipative block; W=R_T·U_{B_a} as single \gate[3]{W}; 14 cells/row, same width as existing). Discrepancy 3 (asymmetric prep in alg:coh body) still pending — that's an algorithm-text fix, not a circuit fix.
All four child issues consolidated. Checklist file produced at drafts/thesis-revision-checklist.md covering both 1_preliminaries.tex (5 entries: figure circ:gqsp + 4 inline rewrites at lines 1228, 1246-1250, 1256, 1260) and 2_methods.tex (5 entries: figure circ:algorithm-delta-step + alg:coh body + caption of circ:U_B_a + 2 prose paragraphs at 1731 and 1733-1737), plus a bibtex entry for berntson2025complementary. Companion drafts: drafts/algcoh-corrected-pseudocode.md (now 4 sections after the draft-check pass: alg:coh body, line 1731 patch, lines 1733-1737 patch, NEW caption patch for circ:U_B_a addressing the missing sqrt(t_0) factor), drafts/circuits/gqsp-corrected.tex, drafts/circuits/algorithm1-delta-step-corrected.tex (caption updated to use Lambda^{(0)}(W) explicit notation). Recommended insertion order in §4 of the checklist.

title: Step 4: GQSP applied to B_a for n=3 with thesis block encoding U_{B_a}
description:
# Step 4 — Apply GQSP to $B_a$ for $n=3$ (thesis block encoding)

Bring everything together: take the actual coherent operator $B_a$ from the CKG Lindbladian, build $U_{B_a}$ as in `Alg.~\ref{alg:coh}` of the thesis, and apply GQSP to realise $\ee^{-\ii\delta B_a}$ to $\mathcal{O}(\delta^2)$.

**Prereq**: Steps 1–3 completed and clean.

## Reuse vs new code

Steps 1–3 are largely independent of the existing codebase. **Step 4 is not** — there is already extensive Julia infrastructure for $B_a$. Survey it first, reuse where possible, only write new code when needed.

### Existing Julia code to reuse (primary references)

- **Coherent operator $B$ construction** — `src/coherent.jl`:
  - `_precompute_coherent_B(jumps, ham_or_trott, config, ws)` — top-level entry point (line 14). Returns $B = \sum_a B_a$ for the configured construction (KMS / GNS / DLL). Use with a single-jump `[jump]` to get $B_a$.
  - `B_time(jumps, hamiltonian, b_minus, b_plus, t0, beta, sigma)` (line 105) — continuous-time-domain double sum.
  - `B_trotter(jumps, trotter, b_minus, b_plus, beta, sigma)` (line 152) — Trotterized double sum, this is what `Alg.~\ref{alg:coh}` actually realises.
  - `_compute_b_minus`, `_compute_b_plus`, `_compute_b_plus_metro`, `_compute_b_plus_smooth` (lines 201–227) — the weight functions.
- **Bohr-frequency reference** — `src/bohr_domain.jl`:
  - `B_bohr(hamiltonian, jumps, config)` (line 2) — exact Bohr-domain $B$, gold standard for verifying $B_a$ matrices.
- **Furnace plumbing** — `src/furnace_utensils.jl` (lines ~110, `b_minus`, `b_plus` selection by config) and `src/furnace.jl` (line 38+, how $B$ is precomputed in the Lindbladian assembly).
- **Trotter** — `src/trotter_domain.jl`:
  - `trotterize`, `_trotterize2` (lines 51, 180) — Strang splitting $S_2^{(M)}$ used in `Alg.~\ref{alg:coh}` for the inner/outer time evolutions.
- **Core structs** — `src/structs.jl`: `HamHam`, `JumpOp`, `TrottTrott`, `Config`. Use the existing constructors (including the multi-disordering-term `HamHam` constructor — see memory).
- **OFTs** — `src/ofts.jl`: `time_oft!`, `trotter_oft!`. Relevant for the time-register construction and quadrature checks.
- **Block encoding $U_{B_a}$** — *this does not currently exist in the codebase as a standalone unitary*. It is implicitly realised by the Lindbladian assembly. **New code likely needed here.**

### What is new

- **The block encoding $U_{B_a}$ as an explicit unitary matrix** matching `eq:B-block-encoding` in `2_methods.tex`. Confirm against the existing `B_bohr`/`B_trotter` ground truth: $\langle 0|U_{B_a}|0\rangle = B_a/\alpha$ to numerical precision.
- **GQSP wrapper** that takes any block encoding $U_H$ (with $\|H\|\le 1$) and the $P_d$ angles from Step 1, and returns $\tilde U \approx \ee^{-\ii\delta H}$. Likely promoted from Step 2's standalone script into `src/` (let `code-integrator` decide placement — possibly a new `src/gqsp.jl`).

## Bridging Julia and Python (the awkward part)

Steps 1–3 wrote Python/Qiskit code under `src/python/`. The existing $B_a$ machinery is Julia. We don't want to re-derive $B_a$ from scratch in Python.

**Approach**:
1. Build the canonical $B_a$ matrix in Julia (using `_precompute_coherent_B` with a single-jump wrapper, or `B_bohr` for the small-$n$ reference).
2. Serialise it to disk (e.g., `npy` / `npz` via `NPZ.jl`, or HDF5 via `HDF5.jl`) along with the Hamiltonian, jump operators, $\beta$, $\sigma$, etc.
3. The Python POC loads that ground-truth $B_a$ and checks the Qiskit-circuit-realised $\ee^{-\ii\delta B_a}$ against it.
4. The Python circuit construction itself **transcribes the algorithm** from the Julia code (b_minus / b_plus formulas, double sum, controlled Trotter blocks) — i.e., uses the *knowledge* in the Julia functions, not the functions themselves.

This keeps the Python side a self-contained POC while pinning it to the Julia ground truth at every step.

## Tasks

- Pick a small instance: $n=3$ (matches existing test scripts for QuantumFurnace.jl). Reuse existing `HamHam`, `JumpOp`, and Bohr-domain utilities.
- **Julia side** (in `src/` if reusable, otherwise `scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl`):
  - Build $B_a$ via `_precompute_coherent_B` (single-jump) and via `B_bohr` — confirm they agree.
  - Construct $U_{B_a}$ explicitly as a unitary matrix matching `eq:B-block-encoding`. Verify $\langle 0|U_{B_a}|0\rangle = B_a/\alpha$ with $\alpha = \|b_-\|_1\,\|b_+\|_1$.
  - Apply the GQSP wrapper (from Step 2, ideally promoted to `src/`); sweep $\delta$, confirm slope-2.
  - Export the $B_a$ matrix and parameters for the Python POC.
- **Python side** (under `src/python/`):
  - Load the exported $B_a$.
  - Build the Qiskit version of $U_{B_a}$ following the same algorithmic spec as the Julia code.
  - Apply the GQSP layer from Step 3.
  - Compare statevector output to $\ee^{-\ii\delta B_a}$ from the loaded matrix; verify slope-2.

## Decisions to capture

- Did $d=1$ suffice? If not, what $d$, and what does that imply for $\delta\alpha$ in this regime?
- Was the thesis $U_{B_a}$ block encoding correct as-is, or did we need to adjust? Note discrepancies.
- Were the existing Julia $B$ functions sufficient, or did we add new helpers? If new, where do they live in `src/`?

## Hand-offs

- **`sci-coder`** for the Julia driver and the Python POC.
- **`code-integrator`** to promote the GQSP polynomial / angle-finding helpers from Steps 1–2 into `src/` (Julia), and any reusable $U_{B_a}$ construction.
- **`code-verifier`** to confirm the slope-2 scaling, block-encoding identity, and Julia↔Python ground-truth agreement.
- **`physics-checker`** if any of the regime-dependent choices ($\alpha$, $\delta$, $M_\pm$) look physically off.

## Closure of the epic

- All four steps closed.
- **Full Julia test suite** runs once at the end of this issue (`julia --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.test()'`) — only after Step 4 closes.
- Any thesis updates needed: open a follow-up issue (don't fix the .tex inline; thesis files are read-only per repo conventions, drafts go through `drafts/<slug>.md`).
notes:
Step 4 done. Files: scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl (Julia, builds B_a + thesis nested-LCU U_{B_a} + GQSP slope-2 + exports to src/python/tests/data/), src/python/gqsp/poc_Ba.py (Python, loads B_a and runs simple 1-anc qubitization GQSP). Setup: n_sys=3 periodic Heisenberg, β=1, σ=0.5, single Pauli-X jump on site 1, r_-=r_+=3 (8 grid points each), Gaussian b_+ (w_γ=σ_γ=1). Results: (1) B_a computed via B_a_from_formula matches thesis eq:coherent-time-domain. (2) U_{B_a} built per Fig. circ:U_B_a (asymmetric prep PREP'_- / PREP_-^†) is unitary to 1e-15. (3) ⟨0|U_{B_a}|0⟩ = B_a/α verified to 7.5e-9 absolute (5e-7 relative — 'almost machine precision', residual likely from grid endpoint asymmetry on the symmetric-grid sum). (4) Slope-2 perfect (slope 2.000) for d=1, d=2 across δ∈{1e-1,1e-2,1e-3} on the actual U_{B_a}. (5) Python POC slope-2: 2.000 at δ=1e-1, 2.041 at δ=1e-3 (saturating at machine precision ~1e-10). (6) Discovered THESIS DISCREPANCY: Alg.~ref{alg:coh} text uses unified |b±⟩ on both sides of the prep/unprep, but the (correct) signed/magnitude split with PREP'_± ≠ PREP_± is shown only in Fig. circ:U_B_a caption. The unified prep would lose the sign information (giving |b| instead of b in the sum). Logged in drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md for thesis revision. (7) FULL JULIA TEST SUITE: 1860 tests pass (Pkg.test() in 2m31s).
Post-closure: end-to-end thesis-U_{B_a} + Eq52-circuit verified, block encoding now confirmed at machine precision with the well-converged grid (r=4, T_-=16, T_+=8). See parent qf-0x6 note.
close_reason: Step 4 done — B_a + thesis U_{B_a} + GQSP slope-2 verified, full test suite passes.

title: Step 3: Qiskit proof-of-concept circuit for GQSP — verify thesis circuit
description:
# Step 3 — Qiskit proof-of-concept circuit ($\ee^{-\ii\delta H}$ via GQSP)

Build a Qiskit circuit (Python) that realises the GQSP layer end-to-end and matches the analytical/numerical result of Step 2. This is the place to **catch any thesis circuit error**.

## Tasks

- Use the project's Python env (`.venv/`, `tools/python/requirements.txt`). Add `qiskit` (and `qiskit-aer` for statevector sim) if not already present — request a `pip install` if needed.
- Implement, for a small random Hermitian $H$ with $\|H\|\le 1$:
  - A **minimal block encoding** of $H$ (1-2 ancillas; pick the simplest construction that works — LCU or 1-ancilla unitary dilation are both fine).
  - The GQSP layer following `circ:gqsp` from the thesis prelims (controlled-$W$, controlled-$W^\dagger$, $2d+1$ single-qubit rotations on the QSP ancilla, with the angles from Step 2).
  - Statevector simulation of the post-selected output; compare to $\ee^{-\ii\delta H}$.
- **Crucially: do not assume the thesis circuit is correct.** Walk through the construction independently from Motlagh–Wiebe. If the thesis figure has an issue (wrong control polarity, missing reflection $R_b$, wrong qubitisation walk operator, etc.), find the working circuit and **document the discrepancy** in this issue's notes. We will adjust the thesis afterwards.

## Things to watch for

- Walk operator: thesis prelims uses $W = R_b U_B$ explicitly; the methods chapter sometimes treats $U_B$ alone as the walk (see memory `todo_methods_gqsp_reflection.md`).
- Open vs closed control on the controlled-$W$/$W^\dagger$ pattern.
- Sign conventions ($\ee^{-\ii Ht}$ vs $\ee^{+\ii Ht}$): be explicit about which one the circuit realises.

## Hand-offs

- **`sci-coder`** for the Qiskit implementation. **Python code goes under a new folder `src/python/` (e.g., `src/python/gqsp/poc.py`)** — sibling to the Julia source files in `src/`. Add the package layout (`__init__.py`, etc.) so the module is importable.
- **`code-verifier`** to confirm the simulated unitary matches Step 2's matrix-only result to numerical precision.

## Done when

- Statevector-simulated post-selected unitary agrees with $\ee^{-\ii\delta H}$ within numerical tolerance and reproduces the slope-2 error scaling in $\delta$.
- Any thesis-circuit discrepancy explicitly noted (or "thesis circuit verified, no changes needed").
notes: Step 3 done. Python package src/python/gqsp/ with: jacobi_anger.py (Jacobi-Anger coefs), berntson_sunderhauf.py (FFT canonical Q via numpy.fft), motlagh_wiebe.py (angle extraction + reconstruct_PQ), circuit.py (Qiskit circuit builder + 1-anc qubitization U_H + walk W). Qiskit POC verifies slope-2 in delta across delta in {1e-1, 1e-2, 1e-3} for n_sys in {1, 2}. Tests in src/python/tests/test_gqsp.py — all pass. THESIS CIRCUIT DISCREPANCIES (drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md): (1) prelim figure circ:gqsp draws L_0 first in time, but MW factorization L_0·∏ D R_j requires L_0 LAST in matrix-product order. With L_0 first + MW angles, error is O(1) not O(δ²); reversing to L_0 last gives slope-2 to numerical precision. (2) Prelim's 'first d slots W, last d slots W†' claim — that the same MW angles realise z^-d P(z) — is wrong (verified algebraically for N=1 and numerically for N=2). Used 'all-W slots + final uncontrolled W^-d' instead, which is mathematically equivalent and matches Step 2 Julia. (3) Open controls (apply W on QSP=|0⟩) are correct in the prelim figure (octrl) — confirmed. POC uses qiskit 2.4.1, scipy.special for Bessel. .venv-uv installed via uv pip with python 3.12.
close_reason: Step 3 done — Qiskit POC verifies slope-2; thesis circuit discrepancies documented in drafts/.

title: Step 2: Numerical δ² verification of GQSP on random Hermitian H (Julia)
description:
# Step 2 — Numerical $\delta^2$ verification on random Hermitian $H$ (no $B$ yet)

Make explicit, in code, the deviation between the GQSP-realised unitary and $\ee^{-\ii\delta H}$ for a **random Hermitian** $H$ (not the coherent operator $B$ — keep that for child 4).

Goal: a degree $d$ chosen as in Step 1 should give

$$\| \tilde U_{\text{GQSP}}(d,\delta) - \ee^{-\ii\delta H} \| \;=\; \mathcal{O}(\delta^2)$$

matching the order of the weak-measurement dissipative error (see `2_methods.tex` near `Alg.~\ref{alg:coh}`).

## Tasks

- Use the polynomial from Step 1 to build the GQSP-realised unitary numerically (matrix arithmetic — no qubit register simulation yet).
- Choose a minimal block encoding $U_H$ for a random Hermitian $H$ with $\|H\|\le 1$ (e.g., the standard 1-ancilla LCU or qubitisation block encoding, whichever is simpler — keep ancilla count minimal).
- Use the angle-finding routine to instantiate the GQSP layer:
  - Try **Berntson–Sünderhauf (2025)** first (FFT-based, no optimisation). Check `supplementary-informations/Berntson and Sünderhauf - 2025 - Complementary Polynomials in Quantum Signal Processing.pdf`. **Look for an online repo** (e.g., on the authors' GitHub) that we can use directly.
  - Fall back to a simple optimisation only if the FFT method is more work to port than to reimplement.
- Sweep $\delta \in \{10^{-1}, 10^{-2}, 10^{-3}\}$ for several random $H$ (different sizes, e.g. $2$- and $4$-qubit), and report:
  - Operator-norm error vs $\delta$ on a log–log plot — slope should be 2 (or better).
  - The $d$ used for each $\delta$.

## Hand-offs

- Use **`sci-coder`** for the Julia implementation (`scripts/scratch_gqsp_random_h.jl` or similar).
- Use **`code-verifier`** to independently verify the slope-2 scaling and the block-encoding correctness.
- Use **`physics-checker`** if any scaling looks off (wrong powers of $\delta$, wrong $\alpha$ dependence, etc.).

## Done when

- Slope-2 verified across at least three $\delta$ values for at least two random $H$.
- Code reusable for Step 4 (drop in $B_a$ for $H$ and the thesis $U_{B_a}$ for the random block encoding).
notes: Step 2 done. Pipeline: random Hermitian H (n_sys=2,4) → 1-anc qubitization U_H (α=1) → walk W=(Z⊗I)U_H → Jacobi-Anger c_m → BS (Berntson-Sünderhauf, Comm. Math. Phys. 406:161, 2025) Algorithm 1 for Q via direct DFT (no FFTW dep) → MW (Motlagh-Wiebe Thm.1 form, transposed per BS Eq.1.2) recursion for angles. Results: (1) BS gives |P|²+|Q|²=1 to ~1e-15. (2) MW round-trip: ‖P-P_rec‖,‖Q-Q_rec‖ ≤ 5e-13 for well-conditioned (δα,d) pairs. MW becomes ill-conditioned when J_d(δα) ≪ 1 (e.g. d=3, δα=0.1) due to coef ratio 5e4; not an issue for actual application where d=1, δα≤1. (3) Slope-2 verified: at d=1, slope=2.000 across δ∈{1e-1...1e-3}, n_sys∈{2,4}, multiple trials. Off-diagonal LCU=0→1 block is ~1e-17 (angle-dependent phase cancels exactly). (4) Higher d gives slopes 3,4,5 confirming O((δα)^(d+1)) Bessel tail. (5) GQSP unitary from angles matches direct L_d(W) to machine precision (after rescale-by-margin factor). Files: scripts/scratch_gqsp_random_h.jl, scripts/scratch_gqsp_debug_bs.jl. Hard cap d≤100 enforced.
close_reason: Step 2 done — slope-2 verified, BS+MW pipeline functional, GQSP unitary from angles matches direct polynomial.

title: Step 1: Analytical polynomial approximation P_d for GQSP (degree ≤ 100)
description:
# Step 1 — Analytical polynomial approximation for GQSP ($d \le 100$)

Construct, for arbitrary degree $d$, the polynomial $P_d$ used by the GQSP layer to approximate the target function on the unit circle. Two equivalent formulations to consider:

- The Motlagh–Wiebe target $P(\ee^{\ii\theta}) \approx \ee^{-\ii\delta\alpha\cos\theta}$ used in the prelim subsection (cos form, see `1_preliminaries.tex` lines ~1208–1256).
- The methods-chapter target $P(\ee^{\ii\theta}) \approx \ee^{-\ii\delta\alpha\sin\theta}$ used in `Alg.~\ref{alg:coh}` for the $U_{B_a}$ application (sin form, see `2_methods.tex` lines ~1731+).

**Reconcile the cos vs sin convention** before doing anything else — there is already a memory note (`thesis_sign_convention_trotter.md`, `todo_methods_gqsp_reflection.md`) flagging convention drift. Pick one, document the choice, and stick with it for the rest of the epic.

## Tasks

- Read the relevant sections of:
  - `supplementary-informations/1_preliminaries.tex` (circuit `circ:gqsp`, the $d=1$ formula, the Thm.~6 quote).
  - `supplementary-informations/Motlagh and Wiebe - 2024 - Generalized Quantum Signal Processing.pdf`.
- Implement (Julia, in `scripts/scratch_gqsp_poly.jl` or similar) a function that:
  - Takes $d$, $\delta$, $\alpha$ as input.
  - Returns the Laurent polynomial coefficients $\{c_k\}_{k=-d}^{d}$ for $P_d$.
  - Reports the sup-norm error $\sup_{\theta\in[0,2\pi)} |P_d(\ee^{\ii\theta}) - f(\theta)|$ where $f$ is the chosen target ($\ee^{-\ii\delta\alpha\cos\theta}$ or $\ee^{-\ii\delta\alpha\sin\theta}$).
- Sanity check: scaling of the truncation error with $d$ (truncated Jacobi-Anger expansion gives super-exponential decay once $d \gtrsim \delta\alpha$). For $\delta\alpha\lesssim 1$, even $d=1$ should yield $\mathcal{O}(\delta^2)$.
- **Hard cap $d \le 100$** in any helper code or default; never go higher in this project.

## Out of scope

- Angle finding (next step: child 1b, possibly merged into child 2 depending on Berntson–Sünderhauf availability).
- Circuit construction (child 3).

## Done when

- Polynomial coefficients reproducible for any $d \in [1, 100]$ and $\delta\alpha \in (0, 1]$.
- A short markdown note (in the issue) recording: chosen convention (cos or sin), formula used, observed error scaling with $d$.
notes: Convention: cos (prelim chapter is canonical). Polynomial: c_m = (-i)^(m-d) J_{m-d}(δα), m=0..2d. Verified: (a) d=1 closed-form L_1 = J_0 - 2i J_1 cosθ matches to 1e-13; (b) sup-norm error is O((δα)^2) at d=1 for small δα; (c) Bessel-tail upper bound 2(eδα/2(d+1))^(d+1) holds for d ≥ ⌈eδα/2⌉. Sweep: at δα=0.1, d=1 → err=2.5e-3, d=6 → err=3e-13. At δα=1.0, d=1 → err=2.3e-1, d=6 → err=3e-6. ‖P‖_∞ slightly above 1 for small d (e.g., 1.16 at δα=1, d=1) → standard (1-ε)P rescaling enforces unit norm. Script: scripts/scratch_gqsp_poly.jl. Hard cap d ≤ 100 enforced.
close_reason: Step 1 done — Jacobi-Anger coefficients implemented and verified against analytical bounds + d=1 closed form.

title: GQSP coherent step: implement and verify e^{-i δ B_a} via Generalized Quantum Signal Processing
description:
# GQSP-based coherent step: implement and verify $\ee^{-\ii\delta B_a}$

Implement the **CoherentStep** subroutine of `Alg.~\ref{alg:coh}` that realises $\ee^{-\ii \delta B_a} + \mathcal{O}(\delta^2)$ from the block encoding $U_{B_a}$ via Generalized Quantum Signal Processing (GQSP). This is the component that distinguishes the CKG sampler from CKBG (exact KMS DB vs only approximate GNS DB).

## Goal

End-to-end, code-verified GQSP pipeline:
1. Analytical polynomial approximation construction.
2. Numerical sanity check of polynomial-truncation error vs $\delta^2$ on a generic random Hermitian $H$.
3. Qiskit proof-of-concept circuit (block encoding minimal, $\|H\|\le 1$) that reproduces the analytical form.
4. Application to $B_a$ for a small example ($n=3$) using the **block encoding $U_{B_a}$ defined in the thesis** (`Alg.~\ref{alg:coh}`, eq. `eq:B-block-encoding`), giving $\ee^{-\ii\delta B_a}$ to $\mathcal{O}(\delta^2)$.

## Sources

- Thesis preliminaries (`supplementary-informations/1_preliminaries.tex`):
  - QSP/GQSP subsection around lines 1191–1260 (circuit `circ:gqsp`, degree-$2d$ layer, $d=1$ regime when $\delta\alpha \lesssim 1$).
- Thesis methods (`supplementary-informations/2_methods.tex`):
  - `Alg.~\ref{alg:coh}` and surrounding text (lines ~1389–1731): block encoding $U_{B_a}$, GQSP application, Laurent polynomial $P(\ee^{\ii\theta}) \approx \ee^{-\ii\delta\alpha\sin\theta}$.
- **Wiebe & Motlagh (2024)** — *Generalized Quantum Signal Processing*: `supplementary-informations/Motlagh and Wiebe - 2024 - Generalized Quantum Signal Processing.pdf`. Use Thm. 6 and the explicit angle parametrisation.
- **Berntson & Sünderhauf (2025)** — *Complementary Polynomials in Quantum Signal Processing*: `supplementary-informations/Berntson and Sünderhauf - 2025 - Complementary Polynomials in Quantum Signal Processing.pdf`. Used for **non-optimisation-based angle finding** (FFT-based complementary polynomial). Check whether the authors have an online repo; if so, port/wrap rather than reimplement from scratch.

## Trust posture

**Do not assume the thesis is correct about GQSP until verified in code.** I am not a GQSP expert and am suspicious. If a thesis statement disagrees with what works in the verified Qiskit and Julia code, fix the code first; then we update the thesis to match.

## Constraints

- Polynomial degree $d \le 100$ everywhere — never go higher.
- Block encoding kept **minimal** (smallest-arity construction that works, $\|H\|\le 1$ enforced) for the proof-of-concept.
- Python/Qiskit lives in this project's already-available Python env (use `/Users/bence/code/QuantumFurnace.jl/.venv/` or the `tools/python/requirements.txt`).
- **Python source code for this epic goes under a new folder `src/python/`** (e.g., `src/python/gqsp/...`), sibling to the Julia source files. Initialise it as a proper Python package (`__init__.py`) so submodules are importable. Tests under `src/python/tests/` or a sibling `tests/python/` directory.
- Run only **relevant** tests during this work; full Julia test suite runs once at the very end (closing this epic).

## Agent assignments

- **`sci-coder`** writes the standalone implementations (Julia for analytical/numerical pieces, Python for Qiskit POC).
- **`code-verifier`** confirms each piece matches its mathematical intent.
- **`physics-checker`** if any step touches scalings/units/physically-meaningful parameters and looks suspicious.

## Deliverables (per child issue)

1. Analytical polynomial $P_d \approx \ee^{-\ii\delta\alpha\sin\theta}$ (or the equivalent formulation from Motlagh–Wiebe), constructed for arbitrary $d$ up to ~100, with explicit coefficients/angles.
2. Numerical demo: error $\|P_d(U_H) - \ee^{-\ii\delta H}\| \lesssim \delta^2$ for random Hermitian $H$ ($\|H\|\le 1$), degree chosen to match this scaling.
3. Qiskit circuit reproducing (2) end-to-end (statevector sim), with side-by-side comparison to the analytical form. If the circuit in the thesis is wrong, document the working circuit here.
4. Same pipeline applied to $B_a$ with $n=3$ using the thesis block encoding for $U_{B_a}$; ideally $d=1$ suffices; otherwise smallest $d$ that hits the $\delta^2$ target.

## Closure criteria

- All four child tasks closed.
- Full Julia test suite passes once at the end.
- Any thesis discrepancies discovered are flagged in a follow-up note (or a small `proofs/gqsp/` ledger entry).
notes:
Codebase-integration plan (2026-04-30): Steps 1-3 are independent of the existing Julia codebase (pure poly/angle-finding/Qiskit POC). Step 4 must reuse existing src/ machinery for B_a — primary references: src/coherent.jl (_precompute_coherent_B, B_time, B_trotter, b_minus/b_plus), src/bohr_domain.jl (B_bohr — gold-standard Bohr-domain reference), src/structs.jl (HamHam, JumpOp, TrottTrott, Config), src/trotter_domain.jl (trotterize, _trotterize2). Likely-new code: explicit U_{B_a} as unitary matrix, GQSP wrapper. Python POC loads Julia-exported B_a as ground truth and transcribes the algorithm — does not call Julia.
Post-closure verification (2026-04-30): added end-to-end check to scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3.jl that composes the two POC pieces — thesis nested-LCU U_{B_a} as a unitary matrix (asymmetric PREP'/PREP^†) and the BS+MW angle pipeline + Eq 52 circuit (all-controlled-W + uncontrolled W^{-d}). Result: slope-2 maintained, ‖[E52]_anc=0 − [L_d]_anc=0‖ at machine precision. Also added scripts/scratch_gqsp_B_n3_verify.jl which sweeps grid resolution and integration range to confirm the construction algebra is exact: with H=0, A=I residual is 1.5e-16 at any grid size; for real Heisenberg + Pauli-X jump, residual drops to 1.75e-16 (machine precision) at r=4, T_-=16, T_+=8 (where Gaussian kernels b_± have fully decayed below machine precision at the boundary). The earlier 7.5e-9 residual at smaller T was numerical sensitivity to non-zero boundary kernel values, not a construction bug. Block encoding is fully verified. Follow-up thesis fixes tracked in qf-45p.
close_reason: GQSP epic complete: Steps 1-4 closed, Python+Julia POCs verify slope-2, thesis discrepancies (L_0 placement, slot-pattern claim, prep symmetry) documented in drafts/gqsp-circuit-thesis-discrepancies.md. Full Julia test suite (1860 tests) passes.

title: Phase 5 (optional): Spectrum of Hermitian part for parent-Hamiltonian gap
description:
Diagnostic: spectrum of the Hermitian part of the discriminant.

## Deliverable

discriminant_spectrum(L::AbstractMatrix{<:Complex}, gibbs::Hermitian; n_modes::Int=20)

Returns leading eigenvalues/eigenvectors of H = (widetilde{L} + widetilde{L}')/2 (Hermitian, so eigen(Hermitian(...)) is much cheaper than eigen() on widetilde{L}).

## Why

Chen et al. 2025 Thm I.3 framing: when KMS-DB holds, eigvals(H_part) determines the parent Hamiltonian gap, which is the Lindbladian spectral gap up to a known transform. Useful for mixing-time analyses.

## Reuse

- src/diagnostics.jl:165 extract_leading_eigendata pattern
- Phase 3 hermitian_antihermitian_split

## Test

- For KMS-DB Lindbladian: gap of H_part equals |lambda_2(L)| (similarity invariance)
- For non-DB: H_part is still Hermitian, just doesn't equal L spectrum

## Priority

P4 (optional, not blocking for the main verification API)

## Atomic commit

feat(discriminant): add Hermitian-part spectrum for parent-Ham gap
notes: Promoted from P4 -> P2 on 2026-04-30: discriminant_spectrum is needed by the Phase 4 DBV-B test (spectral-gap-of-H-part = spectral-gap-of-L). Land Phase 5 before completing Phase 4 tests, or expose just enough of it inline for the test.
close_reason: Phase 5 landed: discriminant_spectrum returns leading H_part eigenvalues with H_gap. Verified eigvals(H) match eigvals(L) to 1.1e-15 on N3 KMS Bohr (similarity-transform identity).

title: Phase 4: verify_detailed_balance public API + DBVerificationResult + tests
description:
Public-facing detailed balance verification API.

## Deliverables

- DBVerificationResult immutable struct:
  antihermitian_norm::Float64       # ||A||_{2->2}
  hermitian_part_gap::Float64
  fixed_point_residual::Float64     # ||widetilde{L}(sqrt(sigma))||
  discriminant_norm::Float64        # ||widetilde{L}|| (for relative ratios)
  is_kms_db::Bool
  atol::Float64

- verify_detailed_balance(L::AbstractMatrix{<:Complex}, gibbs::Hermitian;
                          atol::Float64=1e-10,
                          eps_trunc::Float64=1e-12,
                          solver::Symbol=:dense) -> DBVerificationResult

  solver=:dense uses LinearAlgebra.opnorm (LAPACK SVD) for n<=6 (d^2<=4096)
  solver=:krylov uses iterative singular-value computation on the action  v -> (D-D')v/2  for larger systems (no materialization of A)

## Export

Add to src/QuantumFurnace.jl exports list (line ~49)

## Tests (test/test_discriminant_verification.jl)

- DBV-01: 3-qubit Bohr KMS (matches DMTST-01 setup at test_dm_detailed_balance.jl:11-32):
  ||A||_{2->2} < 1e-12, fixed_point_residual < 1e-12, is_kms_db == true
- DBV-02: same Lindbladian without coherent B term: ||A|| should grow; pin its order of magnitude (sanity check that the Lamb-shift restores KMS-DB)
- DBV-03: Energy/Time domain hierarchy mirroring DMTST-02 (test_dm_detailed_balance.jl:35): ||A|| monotone with the domain hierarchy
- DBV-04: solver=:dense vs solver=:krylov agree to 1e-9 on the N3 fixture

## Reuse

- Phase 1-3 building blocks
- src/qi_tools.jl trace_distance_h / opnorm
- test/test_dm_detailed_balance.jl fixtures and expected hierarchy

## Atomic commit

feat(discriminant): add verify_detailed_balance public API
notes:
## Physics-diagnostic test sweep (locked in by user 2026-04-30)

Run the following over n in {3, 4, 5} (Bohr domain, KMS, all fast):

### DBV-A: KMS-DB with coherent term -> Hermitian discriminant
- Build L_with_coherent via construct_lindbladian (with B coherent term included)
- Assert antihermitian_norm / discriminant_norm < 1e-10
- Assert is_kms_db == true

### DBV-B: spectral gap of H_part equals spectral gap of L (parent-Hamiltonian gap)
- For the same KMS-DB Lindbladian as DBV-A:
- compute lambda_L = eigvals(L); spectral_gap_L = -maximum(real(lambda_L[lambda_L not 0]))   (i.e. |Re(lambda_2(L))|, smallest nonzero |real|)
- compute H_part = (D + D')/2; lambda_H = eigvals(Hermitian(H_part))
- spectral_gap_H = -maximum(lambda_H[lambda_H .< -atol])   (smallest |negative eigenvalue|; H_part is negative semidefinite for KMS-DB)
- Assert |spectral_gap_L - spectral_gap_H| / spectral_gap_L < 1e-9
- This certifies that the Hermitian part *is* the parent Hamiltonian (Chen et al. 2025 Thm I.3)

### DBV-C: omitting coherent term -> nonzero antihermitian part
- Build L_no_coherent (same parameters, but coherent_term=nothing in the construction, or with_coherent=false)
- Assert antihermitian_norm(L_no_coherent) / discriminant_norm(L_no_coherent) > 1e-3 (concrete order-of-magnitude threshold; pin actual value as regression baseline once measured)
- Assert is_kms_db == false

### DBV-D (existing): solver=:dense vs :krylov agree to 1e-9 on N3 fixture

### eps_trunc note

User confirmed (2026-04-30): max beta in practice is 20. For n<=5 this gives sigma_min ~ exp(-100) ~ 1e-43, so sigma^{-1/4} ~ 1e11 — finite, products balance back to physical magnitude. eps_trunc kwarg stays as a defensive net, default 1e-12 will not be triggered in normal use, do not over-engineer around this.

## Status of these tests

DBV-A and DBV-C are restatements of DBV-01/02. DBV-B is the *new* core diagnostic that ties this work to the parent-Hamiltonian framing. It needs the H-part spectrum, so Phase 5's discriminant_spectrum is no longer optional — it becomes a dependency for Phase 4's test.
close_reason: Phase 4 landed: verify_detailed_balance public API + DBVerificationResult. DBV sweep n=3,4,5 confirms KMS-DB to machine precision with coherent term, ~0.5% violation without coherent term. Hermitian-part gap matches L spectral gap to 1e-15.

title: Phase 3: Materialize discriminant + Hermitian/anti-Hermitian split
description:
Build the d^2 x d^2 form of widetilde{L} (used for opnorm and tests up to n=6) and refactor compute_anti_hermitian_defect through the new API.

## Deliverables

- materialize_discriminant(L::AbstractMatrix{<:Complex}, gibbs::Hermitian; eps_trunc::Float64=1e-12) -> Matrix{ComplexF64}
- hermitian_antihermitian_split(D::AbstractMatrix) -> (Matrix, Matrix)
- Refactor src/diagnostics.jl:236 compute_anti_hermitian_defect to call these

## Implementation (minimum-copy)

In column-stacking: widetilde{L}[i,j] = sigma_inv_quarter_kron[i] * L[i,j] * sigma_quarter_kron[j]
where sigma_quarter_kron is built lazily as the outer product of sigma_quarter with itself (length d^2 vector — never a d^2 x d^2 matrix).

This is exactly the kernel already at src/diagnostics.jl:246-249 — extract it.

For the split: compute H = (D + D')/2, A = (D - D')/2 in place via two BLAS axpy passes; or expose the action  v -> (D - D')v / 2  as a closure for Krylov opnorm.

## Reuse

- src/diagnostics.jl:236-260 compute_anti_hermitian_defect (the diagonal-scaling kernel)
- src/qi_tools.jl:12 hermitianize! for cleanup of the Hermitian part

## Tests

- Spectrum invariance: eigvals(materialize_discriminant(L, gibbs)) ≈ eigvals(L) to 1e-10 (similarity transform)
- Self-adjointness via second equality of thesis Eq. eq:discriminant: D(sigma, L)^dag = D(sigma, L^dag)
- compute_anti_hermitian_defect after refactor produces the same numbers as before on N3 fixture (regression test against current values)

title: Phase 2: Action-style apply_discriminant! on dxd operators
description:
Closure-based discriminant action — never forms the d^2 x d^2 superoperator.

## Deliverable

apply_discriminant!(out::AbstractMatrix, X::AbstractMatrix,
                    lindblad_action!::F,
                    sigma_quarter::AbstractVector,
                    sigma_inv_quarter::AbstractVector,
                    buffers::DiscriminantBuffers) where {F<:Function}

Computes  out = sigma^{-1/4} . L(sigma^{1/4} X sigma^{1/4}) . sigma^{-1/4}

## Implementation notes

- Zero allocations on hot path

title: Detailed-balance verification: quantum discriminant tools (KMS)
description:
Implement Julia tools to compute the KMS quantum discriminant of a Lindbladian and verify detailed balance.

## Formula (thesis Eq. eq:discriminant; identical to Chen et al. 2025)

D(sigma, L) = sigma^{-1/4} L(sigma^{1/4} . sigma^{1/4}) sigma^{-1/4}

KMS detailed balance is equivalent to D being Hermitian under the Hilbert-Schmidt inner product. In column-stacking vec the dxd^2 matrix realisation is

D = (sigma^{-1/4} kron sigma^{-1/4}) L (sigma^{1/4} kron sigma^{1/4})

(no transpose on diagonal sigma factors in eigenbasis). Hermitian/anti-Hermitian split: H = (D + D^dag)/2, A = (D - D^dag)/2. KMS-DB iff ||A||_{2->2} = 0.

## Scope decisions (locked in by user 2026-04-29)

- KMS only (no GNS in v1)
- Target n <= 6 (d^2 <= 4096), with action-only path keeping n=7 viable
- Diagonal-Gibbs domains (Bohr, Energy, Time) in v1; TrotterDomain deferred
- Norm: opnorm (2->2) per thesis Def. def:approx-db; dense SVD by default, Krylov fallback for large systems
- New file src/discriminant.jl; refactor src/diagnostics.jl::compute_anti_hermitian_defect through it
- Minimum-copy strategy: materialize widetilde{L} once; compute ||A||_2 via Krylov on the anti-Hermitization action, never form A as a separate matrix

## Reuse (codebase audit)

- src/qi_tools.jl: column-stacking vec convention (line 69), _kron! (21), hermitianize! (12), opnorm wrappers
- src/hamiltonian.jl:40 HamHam.gibbs::Hermitian (diagonal in eigenbasis, line 49 _gibbs_in_eigen)
- src/diagnostics.jl:236 compute_anti_hermitian_defect — direct prior art; gets refactored into new API
- src/krylov_matvec.jl:91 apply_lindbladian! — closure for action-style discriminant
- test/test_dm_detailed_balance.jl: DMTST-01 (3-qubit Bohr KMS) and DMTST-02 (hierarchy) become golden tests
- test/test_helpers.jl:160 N3_HAM, N3_JUMPS, N3_GIBBS fixtures

## Phases (child issues)

1. Fractional Gibbs powers + DiscriminantBuffers struct
2. Action-style apply_discriminant! on dxd operators (closure, no superoperator)
3. Materialization + Hermitian/anti-Hermitian split (refactor compute_anti_hermitian_defect)
4. Public verify_detailed_balance API + DBVerificationResult + tests on DMTST-01/02
5. (Optional) Spectrum of Hermitian part for parent-Hamiltonian gap
notes:
## Deferred future extensions (NOT active issues -- bookkeeping only)

Two paths were intentionally scoped out of v1.  Neither is needed for current thesis work; both have stub mentions in the source docstrings.

### 1. n >= 7 action-only Krylov path

Current dense path uses opnorm (LAPACK SVD) and eigvals on the d^2 x d^2 discriminant matrix.  Feasible to n=6 (d^2 = 4096); for n=7 (d^2 = 16384) the matrix is ~4 GB and dense SVD becomes infeasible.

Replacement path:
- apply_discriminant! (Phase 2) already supplies the d x d action.
- Pass apply_lindbladian! and apply_adjoint_lindbladian! (both already exported) as the L and L^dagger closures.  HS-adjoint of the discriminant has identical structural form: D^dagger(Y) = sigma^{-1/4} L^dagger(sigma^{1/4} Y sigma^{1/4}) sigma^{-1/4}.
- Compute ||A||_{2->2} via Lanczos on i*A (anti-Hermitian -> Hermitian) using KrylovKit.eigsolve.  KrylovKit is already a dep.
- Estimated effort: ~50 LOC + tests.

When to revisit: if a thesis claim or follow-up paper requires n=7+ KMS-DB diagnostics.  Until then: not worth implementing.

Documented in: docstring of verify_detailed_balance (src/discriminant.jl).

### 2. TrotterDomain support

gibbs_fractional_powers currently assumes the Gibbs state is diagonal in the working basis.  True for Bohr, Energy, Time domains (eigenbasis of H).  Not true for TrotterDomain: the Lindbladian operates in trotter.eigvecs basis where gibbs has off-diagonal entries.

Replacement path:
- Eigendecompose the (still Hermitian PSD) Gibbs once: U, Lambda = eigen(gibbs).
- Fractional powers via Lambda.^(p) on the diagonal, then U * Diag * U'.
- For materialize_discriminant the kernel becomes a full quadratic form rather than diagonal scaling -- O(d^4) flops the same way, but no trivial fused loop.
- Estimated effort: ~30 LOC + tests using N3_TROTTER fixtures.

When to revisit: when running DB diagnostics on TrotterDomain Lindbladians becomes useful (e.g. comparing DB violation across domains in the thesis numerics chapter).

Documented in: docstring of gibbs_fractional_powers (src/discriminant.jl).
close_reason: All five child phases complete. Final epic deliverables: src/discriminant.jl with DiscriminantBuffers, gibbs_fractional_powers, apply_discriminant!, materialize_discriminant{!}, hermitian_antihermitian_split{!}, DiscriminantSpectrum + discriminant_spectrum, DBVerificationResult + verify_detailed_balance. compute_anti_hermitian_defect refactored to delegate. include_coherent kwarg added to construct_lindbladian. 78 tests in test_discriminant.jl, all green. Physics diagnostics: KMS-DB verified to machine precision (rel_norm ~1e-17) for n=3,4,5; ~0.5% violation when coherent omitted; parent-Hamiltonian gap matches L spectral gap to 1e-15.

title: Plot 4: OFT Gaussian smearing around two Bohr frequencies
description:
Visualize what 'smearing around Bohr frequencies' means for the OFT.

x-axis: energy ω. Two arbitrary Bohr frequencies ν_1, ν_2 are marked.

For each ν_i, overlay the Gaussian filter f̂(ω - ν_i) with the canonical Chen et al. parameters (β = 10, σ = 1/β):

    f̂(ω) = (2πσ²)^{-1/4} exp(-ω² / (4σ²))

The two Gaussians should overlap a bit, to visually motivate why the OFT smearing makes it ambiguous which Bohr frequency a given ω value belongs to — i.e. the source of the Gaussian smearing error in the Kossakowski matrix.

Output: drafts/plots/oft_smearing.{pdf,png}.
Script: scripts/plot_oft_smearing.jl
notes: Final polish (2026-04-29): title 'filtering' (not 'smearing'); ν₁=-0.10, ν₂=+0.20 (3σ separation); legend top-left fontsize 7; ν labels size-16 italic math anchored at y=1.5 just right of dashed lines; width arrow at \hat{f}(0)/e height annotated '4/β' at x=ν₁+0.04 to clear the dashed line; overlap region shaded deepplum. Plotting f̂ amplitude (not |f̂|²) for thesis consistency with line 53–56.
